items.title: Cyprus Trademarks - Protecting Brand Value In A Digital & Investor World
items.title_zh: 塞浦路斯商标：数字与投资时代的品牌价值保护
items.title_en: Cyprus Trademarks: Safeguarding Brand Value in Digital and Investment Contexts
items.summary: A strong brand is one of the greatest assets a business can build. Yet many Cyprus companies overlook trademarks, treating the registration of their brand name or logo, as a bureaucratic formality rather than a strategic decision. This can lead to lost market share, investor hesitation, and costly disputes.
items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出塞浦路斯企业常忽视商标注册的战略价值，将其视为官僚程序，导致市场份额流失和投资受阻。作者强调商标是创造竞争优势的法律工具，能防止竞争对手搭便车。内容涵盖商标定义、注册路径（塞浦路斯、欧盟及国际）、常见误区及内部准备检查。旨在指导企业通过正确管理商标资产，实现业务增长、吸引投资并最大化品牌价值。
items.ai_summary_en: This analysis highlights that many Cyprus-based enterprises frequently underestimate the strategic importance of trademark registration, mistakenly treating it as a mere bureaucratic formality. This oversight often results in significant market share erosion, investor hesitation, and expensive legal disputes. The article emphasizes that trademarks function as critical legal instruments establishing competitive advantages and preventing competitors from capitalizing on brand reputation. Although intangible, properly registered trademarks deliver tangible business value by serving as defensive shields against infringement and enabling growth, investment attraction, and market expansion. The guide provides comprehensive insights into trademark definitions, strategic registration pathways across Cyprus, the EU, and internationally, alongside common pitfalls to avoid. It aims to help businesses maximize brand value through effective management and proactive protection strategies.
items.ai_insight_en: Companies operating in Europe should shift focus from purely sales-driven strategies to prioritizing intellectual property as a fundamental corporate asset. Mere formal compliance is insufficient; a robust, layered protection strategy combining national and EU-wide registrations is essential. In digital markets, preemptive defensive registrations are crucial to mitigate risks of brand squatting or rights defects. Such vulnerabilities can severely hinder financing opportunities and lead to prohibitive enforcement costs. Proactive trademark management ensures brand security during international expansion, supports investor confidence, and safeguards long-term commercial interests against competitive encroachment and legal challenges.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["纠正塞企将商标注册视为单纯官僚程序的误区，强调其战略资产属性", "解析商标作为法律锚点，在区分来源、防止侵权及提升商业价值中的作用", "提供从定义、注册路径到避免常见误区的全面指南，助力品牌增值"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Corrects the misconception that trademark registration is merely administrative, emphasizing its role as a core strategic asset for business valuation.", "Explains how trademarks act as legal anchors distinguishing commercial origin, preventing infringement, and enhancing overall business worth.", "Offers a practical guide covering definitions, multi-jurisdictional registration paths, and methods to avoid common errors while maximizing brand value."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Treat trademarks as core assets rather than routine compliance tasks to secure investment readiness and market position.", "Practice note: Implement combined national and EU registration strategies to build a comprehensive, multi-layered protective network.", "What to monitor: Preemptively register defensively in digital channels to prevent brand squatting and avoid costly rights disputes."]
core_points_en: Corrects the misconception that trademark registration is merely administrative, emphasizing its role as a core strategic asset for business valuation.
Explains how trademarks act as legal anchors distinguishing commercial origin, preventing infringement, and enhancing overall business worth.
Offers a practical guide covering definitions, multi-jurisdictional registration paths, and methods to avoid common errors while maximizing brand value.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Treat trademarks as core assets rather than routine compliance tasks to secure investment readiness and market position.
Practice note: Implement combined national and EU registration strategies to build a comprehensive, multi-layered protective network.
What to monitor: Preemptively register defensively in digital channels to prevent brand squatting and avoid costly rights disputes.

items.title: EUIPO SME Fund 2026: Reduce Trademark Costs For SMEs
items.title_zh: EUIPO 2026 中小企业基金：降低商标申请成本
items.title_en: EUIPO SME Fund 2026: Reducing Trademark Registration Costs for Eligible Enterprises
items.summary: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (“EUIPO”) SME Fund 2026 enables eligible small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) to reclaim part of the cost of trademark registration...
items.ai_summary_zh: EUIPO 将于 2026 年 2 月启动中小企业基金，符合条件的企业可报销部分国家及欧盟商标注册费。该举措旨在应对日益激烈的品牌竞争，鼓励中小企业尽早布局知识产权。文章对比了马耳他国内商标与欧盟商标的覆盖范围，强调早期申请对规避冲突及获取全域保护的重要性。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) will launch the SME Fund in February 2026, enabling eligible small and medium-sized enterprises to reclaim a portion of their trademark registration fees. This initiative covers costs for both national applications within Member States and European Union Trademarks (EUTM). The program aims to mitigate rising brand competition by encouraging early intellectual property protection strategies. By offering financial reimbursement, the EUIPO supports businesses in securing their brand identity without significant upfront expenditure. The analysis highlights the strategic advantage of EUTMs, which provide immediate coverage across all twenty-seven EU Member States, contrasting this with single-country filings. Early application is emphasized as a critical measure to avoid potential conflicts, reduce opposition risks, and ensure comprehensive legal protection for brand assets throughout the European market.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国赴欧中小企业应密切关注该基金申请窗口，利用财政补贴降低欧盟商标布局成本。建议结合市场规划，优先选择欧盟商标以实现广域保护，避免因费用顾虑延迟注册而遭遇抢注或侵权纠纷。
items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should evaluate the EUIPO SME Fund as a viable mechanism to lower the financial barriers associated with European brand protection. Leveraging these subsidies allows for more robust IP portfolio management without straining operational budgets. Companies must strategically weigh the cost-efficiency of unitary EU trademarks against individual national registrations to align with their expansion goals. Delaying registration due to cost concerns increases exposure to bad-faith filings and enforcement challenges. Proactive planning ensures that brand assets are secured before market entry, providing a stronger legal foundation for commercial activities and dispute resolution within the EU jurisdiction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["eligible 中小企业可获商标申请费用部分报销，涵盖欧盟及成员国层面", "欧盟商标单次申请即覆盖 27 国，相比单一国家注册更具扩张优势", "提前布局商标能有效降低异议风险，确立品牌独占权并防止侵权"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Eligible SMEs can receive partial reimbursement for trademark application fees at both EU and national member state levels.", "A single European Union Trademark application provides immediate protection across all twenty-seven EU Member States.", "Early trademark filing significantly reduces opposition risks and establishes exclusive rights to prevent infringement."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用 EUIPO 基金补贴，显著降低中国品牌进入欧盟市场的知识产权门槛", "权衡单一国与欧盟商标差异，制定符合扩张战略的高性价比注册方案"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilize EUIPO subsidies to significantly lower the financial threshold for establishing trademark protection in the European market.", "Practice note: Assess the strategic benefits of unitary EU trademarks versus national filings to create a cost-effective registration roadmap.", "What to monitor: Track the specific application windows and eligibility criteria for the 2026 SME Fund to secure timely reimbursements."]
core_points_en: Eligible SMEs can receive partial reimbursement for trademark application fees at both EU and national member state levels.
A single European Union Trademark application provides immediate protection across all twenty-seven EU Member States.
Early trademark filing significantly reduces opposition risks and establishes exclusive rights to prevent infringement.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilize EUIPO subsidies to significantly lower the financial threshold for establishing trademark protection in the European market.
Practice note: Assess the strategic benefits of unitary EU trademarks versus national filings to create a cost-effective registration roadmap.
What to monitor: Track the specific application windows and eligibility criteria for the 2026 SME Fund to secure timely reimbursements.

items.title: La porcelaine de Limoges
items.title_zh: 利摩日瓷器获欧盟首项手工艺地理标志保护
items.title_en: Limoges Porcelain Becomes First EU Industrial and Artisanal Geographical Indication
items.summary: La porcelaine de Limoges sgurtler@inpi.fr 11 mai 2026 Avec l'entrée en vigueur du règlement européen sur les indications géographiques pour les produits artisanaux et industriels le 1er décembre 2025, la porcelaine de Limoges devient ce 15 mai la première indication géographique protégée (IGP) française artisanale et industrielle à être enregistrée au niveau européen auprès de l'Office de l'Union européenne pour la propriété intellectuelle (EUIPO). Cette reconnaissance marque une étape décisive
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月15日，利摩日瓷器成为欧盟《手工业和工业产品地理标志条例》生效后，首个在欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）注册的法国手工艺及工业地理标志（IGP）。该认证基于其自2017年起获得的法国国家地理标志地位，通过简化程序转化而来。此举赋予其在欧盟27国的广泛保护及官方标识使用权，标志着法国传统工艺知识产权保护的重要里程碑，并为其他法国手工艺品的欧盟注册开辟路径。
items.ai_summary_en: On May 15, 2026, Limoges porcelain was registered as the first French artisanal and industrial geographical indication at the European Union Intellectual Property Office. This registration follows the entry into force of the new EU regulation on geographical indications for craft and industrial products in December 2025. Leveraging its existing national status granted by INPI in 2017, the designation underwent a simplified transformation process without an opposition phase. The decision grants comprehensive protection across all twenty-seven EU member states and authorizes the use of the official protected geographical indication logo. This milestone underscores the expanded scope of EU IP protection for traditional crafts and sets a significant precedent for other French artisanal sectors seeking similar recognition under the new regulatory framework.
items.ai_insight_en: The expansion of EU geographical indication protection to include artisanal and industrial goods creates new compliance obligations for global manufacturers. Rights holders exporting ceramics or similar handcrafted items must verify that their labeling does not infringe upon newly protected regional names or specific production methods. Conversely, this regulatory shift offers strategic opportunities for non-EU producers with distinct regional specialties. By navigating the application process for EU geographical indications, these entities can enhance brand equity, secure market differentiation, and strengthen their competitive position within the European single market against imitation products.

items.ai_core_points_en: ["Limoges porcelain is the first craft and industrial product to secure EU geographical indication status under the new regulatory framework.", "The designation utilized a simplified transformation procedure based on prior national recognition, bypassing standard EU opposition phases.", "Registration provides uniform legal protection across all twenty-seven EU member states and permits use of the official geographical indication logo."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Exporters of ceramics and artisanal goods must audit product descriptions to avoid infringing on newly protected EU geographical indications.", "Practice note: Rights holders with unique regional characteristics should evaluate eligibility for EU geographical indication registration to secure brand premium and legal exclus…", "What to monitor: Future registrations under the new craft and industrial GI regulation may expand the list of protected terms requiring compliance checks."]

items.title: Wanneer wordt een bewegingsmerk geweigerd wegens technische functionaliteit?
items.title_zh: 欧盟法院裁定：纯技术功能动态商标将被驳回
items.title_en: EU General Court Rejects Motion Mark for Hinged Window Due to Technical Functionality
items.summary: In January 2026, the General Court of the EU refused the application for a motion mark of a hinged window. The General Court considers that the movement consists only of a technical functional process.
items.ai_summary_zh: 2026 年 1 月，欧盟普通法院维持 EUIPO 决定，驳回德国 Kct 公司关于折叠窗开合动画的动态商标注册申请。法院认定该运动序列仅由技术功能过程组成，缺乏显著性且属于《欧盟商标条例》禁止注册的技术解决方案范畴，旨在防止通过商标权无限期垄断技术特征。
items.ai_summary_en: In January 2026, the General Court of the European Union upheld the EUIPO’s decision to refuse registration of a motion mark filed by German company Kct GmbH & Co. The application depicted an animation of a hinged window opening and closing. The Court determined that this movement sequence consisted exclusively of technical functional processes, thereby lacking inherent distinctiveness. Crucially, the mark fell within the scope of Article 7(1)(e)(ii) of the EU Trade Mark Regulation, which prohibits the registration of signs consisting solely of shapes or characteristics necessary to obtain a technical result. This legal provision aims to prevent companies from securing indefinite monopolies on technical solutions through trademark law, effectively extending the protection period beyond that of patents. Consequently, the Court confirmed that such purely functional animations cannot be registered as trademarks.
items.ai_insight_en: Applicants seeking to register motion marks in the EU must ensure their submissions transcend mere descriptions of technical mechanisms. Purely functional animations depicting standard operational movements are likely to face refusal based on technical functionality grounds. To enhance distinctiveness and mitigate rejection risks, it is advisable to incorporate unique, non-functional visual elements into the design. These may include specific color combinations, artistic stylizations, or distinctive deformations that do not serve a technical purpose. By integrating such aesthetic features, brands can better demonstrate that the mark serves as an indicator of origin rather than just a technical process, aligning with current judicial interpretations regarding the separation of function and brand identity.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["动态商标若仅展示产品技术运作过程，将被视为缺乏显著性", "欧盟法律禁止注册仅为获取技术结果所必需的形状或特征", "法院强调需区分功能性要素与装饰性要素以判定可注册性"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Motion marks displaying only product technical operations are deemed to lack distinctiveness under EU trademark law.", "EU legislation explicitly bans registering shapes or features strictly necessary to achieve a specific technical result.", "Courts emphasize distinguishing between functional elements and decorative aspects when assessing registrability."]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid filing motion marks that merely describe standard product technical workflows to prevent automatic rejection.", "Practice note: Integrate non-functional decorative elements into animation designs to strengthen trademark distinctiveness.", "What to monitor: Judicial trends in distinguishing technical functionality from aesthetic branding in motion mark cases."]
core_points_en: Motion marks displaying only product technical operations are deemed to lack distinctiveness under EU trademark law.
EU legislation explicitly bans registering shapes or features strictly necessary to achieve a specific technical result.
Courts emphasize distinguishing between functional elements and decorative aspects when assessing registrability.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid filing motion marks that merely describe standard product technical workflows to prevent automatic rejection.
Practice note: Integrate non-functional decorative elements into animation designs to strengthen trademark distinctiveness.
What to monitor: Judicial trends in distinguishing technical functionality from aesthetic branding in motion mark cases.

items.title: Going nowhere with a bathing ape: earlier rights in EUTM oppositions
items.title_zh: CJEU裁定：欧盟商标异议中在先权利须在决定时持续有效
items.title_en: CJEU Ruling: Earlier Rights Must Remain Valid at EUIPO Decision in EU Trade Mark Oppositions
items.summary: A Bathing Ape (BAPE) is a famous, luxury, Japanese fashion brand. The eponymous BAPE is sometimes stern and sometimes very cute. The cute BAPE is Baby…
items.ai_summary_zh: 日本品牌BAPE因英国未注册权在异议期间因脱欧失效，遭EUIPO驳回。CJEU在C-337/22 P案中裁定，主张在先权利不仅需在争议商标申请日存在，还必须在EUIPO作出最终决定时保持有效且可执行。若权利在程序中灭失，异议即失败。此判决澄清了法律不确定性，恢复了EUIPO的既有做法，强调权利在整个诉讼期间的稳定性至关重要。
items.ai_summary_en: The Japanese brand BAPE faced dismissal of its EU trade mark opposition after UK unregistered rights became ineffective due to Brexit during proceedings. In Case C-337/22 P, the Court of Justice of the European Union ruled that relying on earlier rights requires them to exist not only at the contested application’s filing date but also to remain valid and enforceable when the EUIPO issues its final decision. If these rights lapse during proceedings, the opposition fails. This judgment resolves legal uncertainty stemming from conflicting EUIPO and appellate court decisions, restoring the established approach that emphasizes the continuous stability of rights throughout the entire litigation period.
items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧维权时，应避免仅依赖易失效的短期或未注册权利。建议优先选择稳定的注册商标作为异议基础，并建立动态监控机制，确保在漫长的异议及上诉期内，在先权利持续有效，防止因权利瑕疵导致维权失败。
items.ai_insight_en: This ruling significantly impacts prosecution strategy by extending risk exposure for opponents throughout lengthy opposition and appeal phases. Practitioners should prioritize stable registered trade marks over short-term or unregistered rights to mitigate validity risks. Establishing dynamic monitoring mechanisms is essential to ensure continuous compliance with renewal deadlines and use requirements. Failure to maintain the underlying right can prove fatal to an opposition, making proactive portfolio management critical for successful enforcement in the EU jurisdiction.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU裁定在先权利须在EUIPO最终决定时仍有效且可执行", "异议期间权利若灭失将直接导致异议失败，风险贯穿全程", "权利人需加强组合管理，确保证书续展及维持使用以防失效"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["CJEU rules earlier rights must remain valid and enforceable at the time of the EUIPO's final decision.", "Oppositions fail if relied-upon rights lapse during proceedings, with risks persisting throughout the case.", "Rights holders must strengthen portfolio management, ensuring renewals and use maintenance to prevent invalidity."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["优先选用稳定注册商标，规避短期或未注册权利的失效风险", "建立异议期权利动态监控机制，确保证书续展及使用合规"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Prioritize stable registered trade marks to avoid invalidity risks associated with short-term or unregistered rights.", "Practice note: Implement dynamic monitoring mechanisms during opposition periods to ensure certificate renewals and use compliance.", "What to monitor: Track the legal status and validity of all earlier rights relied upon until the final EUIPO decision is issued."]
core_points_en: CJEU rules earlier rights must remain valid and enforceable at the time of the EUIPO's final decision.
Oppositions fail if relied-upon rights lapse during proceedings, with risks persisting throughout the case.
Rights holders must strengthen portfolio management, ensuring renewals and use maintenance to prevent invalidity.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Prioritize stable registered trade marks to avoid invalidity risks associated with short-term or unregistered rights.
Practice note: Implement dynamic monitoring mechanisms during opposition periods to ensure certificate renewals and use compliance.
What to monitor: Track the legal status and validity of all earlier rights relied upon until the final EUIPO decision is issued.

items.title: EUIPO Report on IP-Backed Finance Makes Policy Recommendations to Unlock Up To €580 Billion in Innovation Financing
items.title_zh: EUIPO 报告建议释放高达 5800 亿欧元创新融资
items.title_en: EUIPO Report Proposes Policies to Unlock €580 Billion in IP-Backed Innovation Financing
items.summary: Today, the European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) published a study exploring challenges faced by EU small- and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) in obtaining financing by offering intellectual property (IP) as collateral. Set against the backdrop of the EU’s recently launched Savings and Investment Union (SIU) program, the EUIPO’s study identifies several structural barriers preventing SMEs from obtaining IP-backed financing and concludes with a series of policy recommendations desig
items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）发布研究报告，探讨欧盟中小企业以知识产权为抵押获取融资时面临的挑战。该研究结合欧盟储蓄与投资联盟计划，识别了阻碍知识产权融资的结构性障碍，并提出一系列政策建议，旨在缩小中小企业信贷缺口，释放高达 5800 亿欧元的经济价值。
items.ai_summary_en: The European Union Intellectual Property Office (EUIPO) has released a comprehensive study examining the significant challenges EU small and medium-sized enterprises face when attempting to secure financing using intellectual property as collateral. Contextualized within the framework of the EU’s newly launched Savings and Investment Union program, the report identifies critical structural barriers that currently hinder the effective utilization of IP assets for credit purposes. It concludes with a series of targeted policy recommendations aimed at bridging the persistent SME credit gap. These measures are designed to facilitate greater access to capital, potentially unlocking up to €580 billion in economic value for the broader European market by enhancing the viability of IP-backed financial instruments.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应关注欧盟 IP 融资新政，评估将商标专利转化为抵押品的可行性。建议提前规范 IP 资产管理，利用潜在政策红利拓宽融资渠道，降低资金成本。
items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders operating within the European market should closely monitor evolving regulatory frameworks regarding intellectual property valuation and collateralization. As the EUIPO advances policies to integrate IP into mainstream finance, companies must assess the feasibility of converting patents and trademarks into tangible security interests. Proactive management of IP portfolios is essential to maximize eligibility for these emerging financing channels. By standardizing asset documentation and valuation practices now, businesses can better position themselves to leverage potential policy incentives. This strategic preparation helps reduce reliance on traditional equity funding, lowers overall capital costs, and enhances financial resilience through diversified access to debt markets backed by intangible assets.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["EUIPO 发布报告分析中小企业 IP 融资难的结构障碍", "研究旨在配合欧盟储蓄与投资联盟计划解决信贷缺口", "提出政策建议以释放高达 5800 亿欧元的创新资金"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["EUIPO report analyzes structural barriers preventing EU SMEs from successfully leveraging intellectual property rights as collateral for obtaining necessary business financing.", "Study aligns with the EU Savings and Investment Union initiative to address systemic credit gaps facing innovative small and medium-sized enterprises across member states.", "Policy recommendations aim to unlock approximately €580 billion in innovation financing by improving mechanisms for valuing and securing intellectual property assets."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注欧盟 IP 融资政策动向，评估资产抵押可行性", "规范知识产权管理，利用新政红利拓宽融资渠道"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Evaluate the feasibility of using registered trademarks and patents as collateral under new EU financing frameworks to diversify funding sources.", "Practice note: Standardize intellectual property asset management and valuation processes to ensure readiness for potential IP-backed lending opportunities.", "What to monitor: Track legislative developments stemming from the EUIPO report and the Savings and Investment Union program affecting IP collateral standards."]
core_points_en: EUIPO report analyzes structural barriers preventing EU SMEs from successfully leveraging intellectual property rights as collateral for obtaining necessary business financing.
Study aligns with the EU Savings and Investment Union initiative to address systemic credit gaps facing innovative small and medium-sized enterprises across member states.
Policy recommendations aim to unlock approximately €580 billion in innovation financing by improving mechanisms for valuing and securing intellectual property assets.

Practice note: Standardize intellectual property asset management and valuation processes to ensure readiness for potential IP-backed lending opportunities.
What to monitor: Track legislative developments stemming from the EUIPO report and the Savings and Investment Union program affecting IP collateral standards.

brief.headline_en: UKIPO Warns of Serious Risks from Counterfeit Toner and Ink Cartridges
brief.summary_zh: 英国知识产权局警示假冒碳粉危害，指出造假者常免责且不赔偿。全球假冒贸易额高达4670亿美元，多关联有组织犯罪。出口企业需建立合规溯源体系以防范供应链指控；进口商应加强供应商尽调，避免采购不明来源低价耗材，强化品牌保护与防伪措施。
key_points: 假冒碳粉致损严重且造假者常免责不赔
全球假冒贸易达4670亿美元并关联有组织犯罪
需建立合规溯源体系及加强供应商尽职调查
actions: 建立产品全链路合规溯源体系
强化进口供应商背景尽调与审核
升级防伪技术以应对假冒指控风险
watchlist: 欧盟及英国后续执法行动趋势
假冒耗材关联犯罪的司法判例更新
跨境供应链合规监管政策变化
brief.topic_name_zh: 假冒执法 / 品牌保护
brief.summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office has issued a warning regarding the significant harms caused by counterfeit ink and toner cartridges, particularly affecting small businesses. The blog highlights that while often perceived as low-risk, fake print consumables can cause severe operational damage. Global trade in counterfeit goods is estimated at USD 467 billion, frequently linked to serious organized criminal networks. Manufacturers of these fakes typically bear no liability or compensation responsibility for damages incurred. Consequently, businesses must implement strict procurement systems and compare product features to identify fakes on online platforms. Establishing robust IP compliance and traceability systems is essential to prevent involvement in counterfeit supply chains. Furthermore, conducting thorough supplier due diligence helps avoid procuring unverified, low-cost consumab...
key_points_en: Counterfeit print consumables cause serious business damage, yet manufacturers bear no liability or compensation responsibility.
Global counterfeit trade reaches approximately USD 467 billion, frequently linked to serious organized criminal networks.
Businesses must implement strict procurement systems and compare product features to identify fakes on online platforms.
actions_en: Establish strict IP compliance and traceability systems to prevent involvement in counterfeit supply chains.
Conduct thorough supplier due diligence to avoid procuring unverified, low-cost consumables.
Upgrade anti-counterfeiting technologies and verification protocols to mitigate counterfeit allegations.
watchlist_en: Subsequent enforcement trends by EU and UK authorities.
Updates on judicial precedents linking counterfeit consumables to organized crime.
Changes in cross-border supply chain compliance regulatory policies.

items.title: Fake toner, real trouble: protect your printers from counterfeits
items.title_zh: UKIPO警示：假冒碳粉危害大，需强化防伪保护
items.title_en: UKIPO Warns of Serious Risks from Counterfeit Toner and Ink Cartridges
items.summary: Counterfeit ink and toner cartridges can be poor quality and can cause serious problems, especially for small businesses.
items.ai_summary_zh: 英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布博客文章，警示假冒墨盒和碳粉对中小企业造成的严重损害。文章指出全球假冒贸易规模巨大且常关联有组织犯罪，电商平台第三方卖家易成售假渠道。UKIPO联合ICCE等机构开展执法与情报协调，强调造假者不承担赔偿责任。建议企业建立严格采购体系，通过比对正品特征识别假货，以规避法律与经济损失风险。
items.ai_summary_en: The UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) has issued a warning regarding the significant harms caused by counterfeit ink and toner cartridges, particularly affecting small businesses. The blog highlights that while often perceived as low-risk, fake print consumables can cause severe operational damage. Global trade in counterfeit goods is estimated at USD 467 billion, with strong links to organized crime. Online marketplaces facilitate the sale of these items through third-party sellers offering tempting prices. Crucially, counterfeiters cannot be held liable for damages and offer no compensation. The UKIPO collaborates with the Imaging Consumables Coalition of Europe, Middle East and Africa (ICCE) to coordinate enforcement and intelligence efforts against counterfeiting networks.
items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国打印耗材出口企业应警惕被卷入假冒供应链指控。需建立严格的知识产权合规与溯源体系，主动配合品牌方打假。同时，进口商应加强供应商尽职调查，避免采购来源不明的低价耗材，以防因销售假冒产品面临UKIPO执法行动及民事索赔风险。
items.ai_insight_en: Companies operating within European supply chains should prioritize robust intellectual property compliance and traceability mechanisms. Exporters must ensure their distribution channels are not inadvertently used for counterfeit goods, while importers need rigorous supplier due diligence to avoid sourcing unverified, low-cost alternatives. Failure to verify product origins may result in enforcement actions by authorities like the UKIPO and exposure to civil liability claims. Proactive cooperation with brand owners and industry coalitions is essential to mitigate legal risks and protect commercial reputation against allegations of facilitating counterfeit trade.
items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UKIPO警示假冒耗材致损严重，造假者免责且不赔偿", "全球假冒贸易达4670亿美元，常关联有组织犯罪网络", "建议企业完善采购系统，比对正品特征以识别假冒商品"]
items.ai_core_points_en: ["Counterfeit print consumables cause serious business damage, yet manufacturers bear no liability or compensation responsibility.", "Global counterfeit trade reaches approximately USD 467 billion, frequently linked to serious organized criminal networks.", "Businesses must implement strict procurement systems and compare product features to identify fakes on online platforms."]
items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口企业需建立合规溯源体系，防范假冒供应链指控", "进口商应加强供应商尽调，避免采购不明来源低价耗材"]
items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish strict IP compliance and traceability systems to prevent involvement in counterfeit supply chains.", "Practice note: Conduct thorough supplier due diligence to avoid procuring unverified, low-cost consumables.", "What to monitor: Enforcement trends by UKIPO and ICCE regarding counterfeit print materials and online marketplace liabilities."]
core_points_en: Counterfeit print consumables cause serious business damage, yet manufacturers bear no liability or compensation responsibility.
Global counterfeit trade reaches approximately USD 467 billion, frequently linked to serious organized criminal networks.
Businesses must implement strict procurement systems and compare product features to identify fakes on online platforms.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish strict IP compliance and traceability systems to prevent involvement in counterfeit supply chains.
Practice note: Conduct thorough supplier due diligence to avoid procuring unverified, low-cost consumables.
What to monitor: Enforcement trends by UKIPO and ICCE regarding counterfeit print materials and online marketplace liabilities.

news_items.title: Switzerland introduces substantive examinations with Patent Act reform
news_items.title_zh: 瑞士通过专利法修订引入实质审查制度
news_items.title_en: Switzerland Reforms Patent Act: Mandatory Searches and Optional Substantive Examinations
news_items.summary: Key changes in the Swiss Patent Act include a mandatory prior-art search, optional full substantive examination, the acceptance of English-language technical documents and a new appeal system. In March 2024, the Federal Assembly passed a partial revision of the Patent Act, introducing new features to the Swiss patent system. The Swiss government, as supported by […] The post Switzerland introduces substantive examinations with Patent Act reform appeared first on JUVE Patent .
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 2024年3月，瑞士联邦议会通过《专利法》部分修订案。此次改革核心在于引入强制性的现有技术检索，并允许申请人选择是否进行全面的实质审查。此外，新规接受英文技术文件，并建立了新的上诉系统。这一变革标志着瑞士从形式审查向更严格的审查模式转变，旨在提升专利质量与法律确定性，对在该国进行专利布局的企业将产生深远影响。
news_items.ai_summary_en: In March 2024, the Swiss Federal Assembly passed a partial revision of the Patent Act, marking a significant shift in the national intellectual property framework. The reform introduces a mandatory prior-art search for all applications and establishes an optional full substantive examination procedure. Additionally, the updated law accepts technical documents in English and implements a new appeal system. These changes transition Switzerland from a purely formal examination model to a stricter review regime. The primary objectives are to enhance overall patent quality and increase legal certainty for rights holders. This legislative update fundamentally alters the procedural landscape, requiring market participants to adapt their filing strategies to meet higher authorization thresholds and manage increased procedural complexity within the Swiss patent system.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业赴瑞士申请专利需适应新流程，强制检索可能增加前期成本与时间。建议提前规划现有技术分析，利用英文文件便利降低翻译成本。同时，关注实质审查的可选性，根据商业价值权衡投入，以应对更高的授权门槛和潜在的驳回风险。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Market participants filing in Switzerland must adapt to the new mandatory prior-art search requirements, which may increase initial costs and processing times. Applicants should optimize early-stage prior-art analysis and budget planning to accommodate these changes. The acceptance of English technical documents offers an opportunity to reduce translation expenses, provided filings are managed efficiently. Furthermore, parties must carefully evaluate the commercial necessity of opting for substantive examination. Given the higher authorization thresholds and potential for refusal under the new regime, strategic decisions regarding examination requests should align with the specific business value and enforcement goals of each patent portfolio.

news_items.title: Official UPC Year in Review – Five highlights from 2025
news_items.title_zh: UPC官方2025年度回顾：五大亮点解析
news_items.title_en: UPC 2025 Annual Review: Five Key Highlights for Patent Litigants and Rights Holders
news_items.summary: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) recently released its second official Annual Review, a copy of which can be found on the UPC’s website at UPC Annual Revi...
news_items.ai_summary_zh: Bristows律所发布分析，解读统一专利法院（UPC）发布的2025年官方年度回顾报告。该报告是UPC的第二份年度报告，全面梳理了2025年的机构活动、案件趋势及运营发展。文章从中提炼出五个对UPC用户至关重要的亮点，旨在帮助利益相关者更好地理解UPC的最新动态与司法实践走向，为在欧专利诉讼策略提供参考依据。
news_items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court (UPC) has published its second official Annual Review, offering a comprehensive overview of institutional activities, case trends, and operational developments throughout 2025. This report serves as a critical resource for understanding the court's evolving jurisprudence and procedural efficiency. Bristows analyzes the document to extract five significant highlights that are particularly relevant for UPC users. These insights provide legal practitioners and corporate counsel with essential data to navigate the complex landscape of European patent litigation. By examining these key developments, stakeholders can better anticipate judicial directions and refine their strategic approaches to enforcement and defense within the unified system.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and market participants should closely monitor the case trends and procedural shifts outlined in the UPC’s annual review. Particular attention is warranted regarding judicial practices concerning preliminary injunctions and jurisdictional matters. It is advisable to proactively assess patent portfolio risks within Europe and optimize litigation response strategies. Leveraging these insights allows companies to effectively protect their intellectual property rights or mitigate potential infringement allegations. This approach ensures compliance and stability for export operations and broader commercial activities within the European market.

news_items.title: UK Court of Appeal issues guidance on online restrictions in selective distribution systems
news_items.title_zh: 英国上诉法院就选择性分销体系在线限制发布指引
news_items.title_en: UK Court of Appeal Clarifies Legality of Online Restrictions in Selective Distribution
news_items.summary: Restrictions in a selective distribution model affecting online channels or discounting are not automatically illegal but need to be carefully assessed ...
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Deckers诉Up & Running案中裁定，品牌方禁止授权零售商在未品牌折扣网站销售不违反竞争法，推翻竞争上诉法庭原判。法院确认此类在线限制需结合法律与经济背景评估损害程度，而非自动违法。此举虽未放松对转售价格维持等核心限制的执法，但强调品牌应建立透明、客观的分销标准以规避诉讼风险。
news_items.ai_summary_en: The UK Court of Appeal ruled in Deckers UK Limited v Up & Running (UK) Limited that preventing an authorised retailer from selling on unbranded discount websites does not automatically breach competition law. This decision overturns the Competition Appeal Tribunal’s earlier judgment, confirming that restrictions within selective distribution systems require a nuanced assessment of their legal and economic context to establish sufficient harm. The ruling aligns with established case law such as Coty, emphasizing that online sales limitations are not presumed illegal. However, this does not signal leniency towards hardcore restrictions like resale price maintenance, as evidenced by recent significant fines against luxury brands. Rights holders must ensure transparent, objective criteria for distribution networks, focusing on brand positioning and network integrity, to mitigate litigation risks while maintaining compliance with EU and UK competition standards.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出口欧洲的品牌商需警惕选择性分销中的在线限制合规性。建议避免直接禁止线上销售或固定价格，转而制定基于品牌形象和防止搭便车的客观准入标准。完善合同条款与证据留存，可有效应对潜在的反垄断调查与民事诉讼，保障供应链稳定。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating selective distribution systems in Europe must carefully distinguish between permissible qualitative criteria and prohibited hardcore restrictions. While brands retain discretion to protect their image by restricting sales on unbranded discount platforms, they must document objective justifications linked to brand positioning or preventing free-riding. Blanket bans on online sales or any form of resale price maintenance remain high-risk practices subject to severe enforcement actions. Establishing clear, written policies and maintaining robust evidence of compliance can significantly reduce exposure to costly antitrust litigation and regulatory investigations, ensuring supply chain stability and legal certainty across jurisdictions.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["在线销售限制非自动违法，需个案评估法律经济背景及损害程度", "上诉法院推翻初审判决，支持品牌方管控未品牌折扣渠道的合规性", "企业应避免价格固定等核心限制，通过透明标准降低反垄断诉讼风险"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Online sales restrictions in selective distribution are not automatically illegal but require contextual assessment.", "The Court of Appeal overturned the CAT, supporting brand control over unbranded discount channels.", "Companies should avoid hardcore restrictions like price fixing and adopt transparent distribution criteria."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["制定基于品牌定位的客观分销标准，避免 blanket ban 线上销售", "严禁转售价格维持等核心限制，完善合规文档以应对反垄断审查"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Brands must define objective, transparent criteria for online channel access to justify restrictions without infringing competition law.", "Practice note: Avoid blanket online sales bans or price-fixing; instead, base restrictions on brand integrity and prevention of free-riding.", "What to monitor: Enforcement trends regarding hardcore restrictions and judicial interpretations of 'sufficient harm' in digital markets."]
core_points_en: Online sales restrictions in selective distribution are not automatically illegal but require contextual assessment.
The Court of Appeal overturned the CAT, supporting brand control over unbranded discount channels.
Companies should avoid hardcore restrictions like price fixing and adopt transparent distribution criteria.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Brands must define objective, transparent criteria for online channel access to justify restrictions without infringing competition law.
Practice note: Avoid blanket online sales bans or price-fixing; instead, base restrictions on brand integrity and prevention of free-riding.
What to monitor: Enforcement trends regarding hardcore restrictions and judicial interpretations of 'sufficient harm' in digital markets.

news_items.title: Registration-based Enablement: Non-technical
news_items.title_zh: EPO裁定：基于注册的启用属于非技术特征
news_items.title_en: EPO Board Rules Registration-Based Enablement Non-Technical in IoT Smoking System Case
news_items.summary: This page appears to be a security verification checkpoint that temporarily blocks access while validating the incoming request. The system implements protective measures to distinguish legitimate users from automated traffic or potential security threats.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 本案涉及菲利普莫里斯电热吸烟系统专利的异议程序。上诉委员会认定，设备需经互联网主机注册后方可启用的特征（特征1.7），仅反映非技术的商业或行政要求，不贡献于发明的技术特性。即便该要求导致设备运行状态发生物质变化，其底层概念的非技术性质不变。此裁决明确了在评估创造性时，纯粹由商业规则驱动的功能性限制将被排除在技术贡献之外。
news_items.ai_summary_en: This case concerns patent proceedings for Philip Morris’ electrically heated smoking system. The Technical Board of Appeal determined that the feature requiring device enablement only after registration via an Internet host constitutes a non-technical business or administrative constraint. Consequently, this requirement does not contribute to the technical character of the invention. The Board clarified that even if implementing such a registration mandate causes a material change in the device’s operational state, the underlying concept remains non-technical. This decision establishes that functional limitations driven purely by commercial rules are excluded from technical contribution assessments when evaluating inventive step under European patent law.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧申请IoT及智能硬件专利时，应避免将单纯的商业逻辑（如付费解锁、注册激活）作为核心技术特征。撰写时需深入挖掘实现这些功能背后的具体技术改进（如通信协议优化、安全加密算法），确保权利要求聚焦于解决技术问题，以防因缺乏技术贡献而在异议或无效程序中被驳回。
news_items.ai_insight_en: For applicants in the IoT and smart hardware sectors, relying on pure commercial logic, such as paid unlocks or registration activation, as core technical features risks rejection for lack of technical contribution. Drafting strategies should instead focus on specific technical improvements enabling these functions, such as optimized communication protocols or security encryption algorithms. Claims must address concrete technical problems to withstand validity challenges. This approach ensures that the patent portfolio maintains robustness during opposition or invalidation proceedings by anchoring inventions in technical rather than administrative solutions.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["注册后启用被视为非技术商业约束，不赋予发明技术特性", "实施商业规则导致的设备状态改变不改变其非技术本质", "在评估创造性时，纯商业或行政要求不被视为对技术的贡献"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Registration-based enablement is classified as a non-technical business constraint lacking technical character.", "Material changes in device state due to commercial rules do not alter the non-technical nature of the concept.", "Purely commercial or administrative requirements are excluded from technical contributions in inventive step assessments."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免将纯商业逻辑作为核心技术创新点申报欧洲专利", "聚焦实现功能的具体技术改进以确立专利的技术贡献"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid claiming pure commercial logic like registration as core innovation for European patents.", "Practice note: Focus claims on specific technical implementations, such as protocol optimizations, to establish technical contribution.", "What to monitor: Monitor EPO trends excluding administrative constraints from technical character assessments in IoT cases."]
core_points_en: Registration-based enablement is classified as a non-technical business constraint lacking technical character.
Material changes in device state due to commercial rules do not alter the non-technical nature of the concept.
Purely commercial or administrative requirements are excluded from technical contributions in inventive step assessments.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid claiming pure commercial logic like registration as core innovation for European patents.
Practice note: Focus claims on specific technical implementations, such as protocol optimizations, to establish technical contribution.
What to monitor: Monitor EPO trends excluding administrative constraints from technical character assessments in IoT cases.

news_items.title: Copyright royalty rates excessive of economic value may be abusive of dominant position, rules CJEU against Czech CMO
news_items.title_zh: CJEU：忽视入住率的版权费率过高或构成滥用市场支配地位
news_items.title_en: CJEU Rules Ignoring Hotel Occupancy in Copyright Fees May Abuse Dominant Position
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟法院裁定捷克集体管理组织OSA向酒店收取固定版权费未考虑房间入住率，可能违反TFEU第102条构成滥用支配地位。法院强调版权许可费应与被许可人实际获得的经济价值成比例，若行政上可行，忽略入住率等实际使用情况的定价属不公平高价。该裁决要求版权收费机制必须反映真实经济价值，对欧盟内版权许可实践具有深远影响。
news_items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the EU ruled that a Czech collective management organisation’s practice of charging flat-rate copyright fees without considering hotel room occupancy may constitute an abuse of dominant position under Article 102 TFEU. The Court emphasized that royalty rates must be proportionate to the actual economic value received by licensees and the specific use of protected works. Ignoring factual usage indicators, such as occupancy rates or seasonal closures, creates a disconnect between price and value. Where administratively feasible, pricing models must reflect these realities to avoid being classified as unfairly high. This decision significantly impacts copyright licensing practices across the EU, requiring collective management organisations to align fee structures with the genuine commercial benefit derived by users of copyrighted material.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧运营酒店、餐饮及娱乐设施的中国企业应重新审查现有版权许可协议。若支付固定费用且未挂钩实际使用情况，可依据此判例主张费率不公并寻求调整。建议评估历史缴费记录，必要时通过谈判或法律途径争取更公平的计费方式，以降低合规成本并规避反垄断风险。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders and licensees operating in the EU should review existing copyright licensing agreements, particularly those involving fixed fees disconnected from actual usage metrics like occupancy. This CJEU precedent provides a basis to challenge unfair pricing structures that fail to reflect the real economic value derived from protected works. Stakeholders may leverage this ruling during negotiations to secure more equitable, usage-adjusted fee arrangements. Conducting an audit of historical payments against actual operational data can help identify potential overcharges. Proactively adjusting licensing terms mitigates antitrust exposure and ensures compliance with evolving standards linking copyright remuneration to tangible market benefits.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU裁定忽略入住率的固定版权费率可能构成滥用市场支配地位", "版权许可费必须与被许可人实际获得的经济价值及作品使用情况成比例", "若行政可行，集体管理组织需采用基于实际使用（如入住率）的计费方法"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Flat-rate copyright fees ignoring hotel occupancy may abuse dominant position under EU competition law.", "Royalty rates must be proportionate to the actual economic value and specific use of protected works.", "Collective management organisations should adopt usage-based billing methods where administratively feasible."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["在欧中企应审查版权许可费是否与实际使用情况挂钩以规避反垄断风险", "可依据CJEU判例主张固定费率不公，通过谈判或诉讼争取合理计费方式"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Licensees can challenge fixed fees lacking usage links to mitigate antitrust risks.", "Practice note: Use CJEU precedent to negotiate fairer, usage-based copyright royalty adjustments.", "What to monitor: Enforcement trends regarding disproportionate royalties by collective management organisations."]
core_points_en: Flat-rate copyright fees ignoring hotel occupancy may abuse dominant position under EU competition law.
Royalty rates must be proportionate to the actual economic value and specific use of protected works.
Collective management organisations should adopt usage-based billing methods where administratively feasible.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Licensees can challenge fixed fees lacking usage links to mitigate antitrust risks.
Practice note: Use CJEU precedent to negotiate fairer, usage-based copyright royalty adjustments.
What to monitor: Enforcement trends regarding disproportionate royalties by collective management organisations.

news_items.title: Technology licensing under the revised TTBER: clearer guidance for pools, FRAND and collective negotiations
news_items.title_zh: 修订版TTBER下技术许可：专利池、FRAND及集体谈判指南更清晰
news_items.title_en: Revised EU TTBER: Enhanced Guidance on Patent Pools, FRAND, and Collective Negotiations
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟委员会于2026年4月16日通过修订版《技术转移集体豁免条例》(TTBER)及配套指南，将于5月1日生效。新规保留原有安全港结构，但细化了针对专利池、标准必要技术(SEP)许可及集体谈判的合规指导。对于涉及复杂许可模式的企业，需重点评估协议结构以降低反垄断风险，确保符合TFEU第101(3)条要求。
news_items.ai_summary_en: On 16 April 2026, the European Commission adopted the revised Technology Transfer Block Exemption Regulation (TTBER) and accompanying Guidelines, effective 1 May 2026. This new framework replaces the 2014 rules, maintaining the established safe harbour structure under Article 101(3) TFEU for agreements meeting market share thresholds and avoiding hardcore restrictions. Significantly, the revised Guidelines provide detailed competition law guidance for modern licensing structures, including technology pools, standard-essential technology (SEP) licensing, data licensing, and licensing negotiation groups. For entities utilizing complex models such as portfolio licences or collective negotiations, compliance now requires careful structural assessment to mitigate antitrust risks while leveraging the block exemption.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国科技企业应密切关注新规对SEP许可及专利池的合规要求，特别是在参与集体谈判时。建议重新审查现有许可协议，确保不包含核心限制条款，并利用新指南优化FRAND谈判策略，以降低在欧面临的反垄断调查与诉讼风险。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders involved in technology licensing should closely monitor the updated compliance requirements for SEP licensing and patent pool participation, particularly regarding collective negotiation mechanisms. It is advisable to review existing licensing agreements to ensure they do not contain hardcore restrictions that could jeopardize block exemption status. Furthermore, parties should utilize the new Guidelines to refine their FRAND negotiation strategies and structural documentation. This proactive approach helps minimize exposure to antitrust investigations and litigation within the EU jurisdiction, ensuring that complex licensing models remain compliant with current competition law standards.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["修订版TTBER及指南于2026年5月1日生效，取代2014年框架", "新增对专利池、数据许可及标准必要技术许可的详细竞争法指导", "企业需优化复杂许可协议结构以规避反垄断风险并享受安全港保护"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["The revised TTBER and Guidelines enter into force on 1 May 2026, replacing the previous 2014 regulatory framework.", "New provisions offer specific competition law guidance for patent pools, data licensing, and standard-essential technology arrangements.", "Companies must optimize complex licensing agreement structures to ensure antitrust compliance and qualify for safe harbour protection."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重新审查SEP许可协议以符合新规反垄断合规要求", "利用新指南优化专利池参与及集体谈判策略"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Review SEP licensing agreements to align with new antitrust compliance requirements under the revised TTBER.", "Practice note: Leverage updated guidelines to optimize participation in patent pools and structure collective negotiation strategies effectively.", "What to monitor: Evolving enforcement priorities regarding hardcore restrictions and compliance in complex technology transfer arrangements."]
core_points_en: The revised TTBER and Guidelines enter into force on 1 May 2026, replacing the previous 2014 regulatory framework.
New provisions offer specific competition law guidance for patent pools, data licensing, and standard-essential technology arrangements.
Companies must optimize complex licensing agreement structures to ensure antitrust compliance and qualify for safe harbour protection.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Review SEP licensing agreements to align with new antitrust compliance requirements under the revised TTBER.
Practice note: Leverage updated guidelines to optimize participation in patent pools and structure collective negotiation strategies effectively.
What to monitor: Evolving enforcement priorities regarding hardcore restrictions and compliance in complex technology transfer arrangements.

news_items.title: Bösgläubigkeit (bad faith) in trademark filings: A growing risk for businesses
news_items.title_zh: 德国商标申请中的恶意注册：企业日益增长的风险
news_items.title_en: Bad Faith Trademark Filings in Germany: Rising Risks and Strategic Implications
news_items.summary: Discover how the concept of ‘Bösgläubigkeit’ (bad faith) operates in German law and how to avoid filing or being infringed by bad‑faith filings.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 文章分析德国商标法中“恶意”（Bösgläubigkeit）概念的应用与风险。德国专利商标局指出，自2025年中以来，针对未注册商标的恶意申请激增，常利用AI工具识别目标并通过电商平台施压。依据《德国商标法》，缺乏真实使用意图且旨在阻碍竞争或获取不当利益的申请将被驳回或宣告无效。该认定需综合评估申请人主观意图与客观情况，受欧盟判例影响显著。
news_items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines the application of 'Bösgläubigkeit' (bad faith) within German trademark law. The German Patent and Trade Mark Office reports a surge in bad-faith applications since mid-2025, often targeting unregistered signs identified via AI tools to exert pressure through online platforms. Under the German Trademark Act, applications lacking genuine use intent and aimed at obstructing competition or securing undue advantages are subject to refusal or invalidation. Determining bad faith requires a comprehensive assessment combining subjective intent with objective circumstances. This legal framework is significantly influenced by EU case law, aligning national practice with broader European standards regarding abusive trademark registrations and their impact on market participants.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 对在欧中国企业而言，应尽早完成核心品牌在德注册以确立权利基础，避免被抢注。同时需建立主动监控机制，警惕竞争对手利用恶意注册通过电商平台快速下架产品。面对此类指控，应积极收集在先使用证据，援引德国商标法关于恶意的规定提起无效宣告请求，维护市场地位。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should secure early trademark registration in Germany to establish robust rights bases against potential squatting. Implementing active monitoring systems is crucial to detect potentially bad-faith filings that could lead to rapid product removals on e-commerce platforms. If facing such allegations or actions, rights holders must diligently collect evidence of prior use. Leveraging German statutory provisions on bad faith, parties can file for invalidation declarations to protect their market position and counteract abusive strategies employed by competitors seeking unfair competitive leverage through intellectual property mechanisms.

news_items.title: How to plan successful IP mediation
news_items.title_zh: 如何规划成功的知识产权调解
news_items.title_en: Strategic IP Mediation: Leveraging EUIPO and UPC Mechanisms for Efficient Dispute Resolution
news_items.summary: Mediation is an increasingly attractive option to resolve IP disputes, especially for small and medium-sized enterprises, individuals and universities
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出调解因高效、保密及商业导向优势，成为解决IP纠纷的优选。WIPO数据显示2025年案件激增42%，中小企业占比高。欧盟层面，EUIPO扩大调解服务范围，UPC将于2026年设立专利调解中心。调解由中立专家主持，灵活处理跨法域冲突，提供法院无法给予的商业解决方案，有助于建立双赢局面。
news_items.ai_summary_en: Mediation has emerged as a preferred method for resolving intellectual property disputes, offering efficiency, confidentiality, and commercially oriented solutions. Data from the World Intellectual Property Organization indicates a significant 42% increase in administered cases in 2025, with small and medium-sized enterprises representing a substantial portion of participants. In Europe, the European Union Intellectual Property Office has expanded its mediation services to cover all inter partes proceedings for EU trademarks and designs. Furthermore, the Unified Patent Court is set to inaugurate its Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in 2026. These developments provide flexible frameworks for handling cross-jurisdictional conflicts, enabling parties to achieve mutually beneficial outcomes that traditional litigation often cannot deliver.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧面临IP纠纷时，应优先考虑利用EUIPO及即将成立的UPC调解机制。相比传统诉讼，调解成本更低且能保护商业秘密，尤其适合中小企业。建议提前在合同中加入调解条款，并熟悉WIPO等机构的规则，以灵活应对侵权或许可谈判，避免高昂 litigation 风险。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in Europe should strategically consider alternative dispute resolution mechanisms to manage IP risks effectively. The expansion of EUIPO mediation services and the upcoming UPC Patent Mediation Centre provide accessible, cost-effective forums for resolving conflicts without the adversarial nature of court proceedings. This approach is particularly advantageous for protecting trade secrets and maintaining business relationships. Rights holders are advised to incorporate mandatory mediation clauses into licensing and partnership agreements proactively. Familiarity with WIPO and institutional rules allows for more agile responses to infringement claims or negotiation deadlocks, thereby mitigating the financial and reputational risks associated with prolonged litigation.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["调解具高效保密优势，WIPO案件量增42%，中小企参与度高", "EUIPO扩大调解范围，UPC将于2026年设立专利调解仲裁中心", "调解灵活处理跨法域冲突，提供超越诉讼的商业双赢方案"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["WIPO reported a 42% surge in IP mediation cases in 2025, with SMEs and individuals comprising the majority of involved parties.", "The EUIPO expanded its mediation scope to all inter partes trademark and design proceedings, while the UPC will launch its patent mediation centre in 2026.", "Mediation offers a flexible, confidential alternative to litigation, facilitating cross-border resolutions and creating commercial win-win opportunities."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["优先利用EUIPO及UPC调解机制降低在欧维权成本与风险", "建议在合同中预设调解条款，灵活运用ADR解决跨境IP争议"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilizing EUIPO and upcoming UPC mediation mechanisms can significantly reduce enforcement costs and litigation risks for rights holders in Europe.", "Practice note: Draft contracts with pre-agreed mediation clauses to ensure access to efficient, confidential dispute resolution processes when conflicts arise.", "What to monitor: Track the operational rollout of the UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in 2026 and evolving procedural guidelines from major ADR providers."]
core_points_en: WIPO reported a 42% surge in IP mediation cases in 2025, with SMEs and individuals comprising the majority of involved parties.
The EUIPO expanded its mediation scope to all inter partes trademark and design proceedings, while the UPC will launch its patent mediation centre in 2026.
Mediation offers a flexible, confidential alternative to litigation, facilitating cross-border resolutions and creating commercial win-win opportunities.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilizing EUIPO and upcoming UPC mediation mechanisms can significantly reduce enforcement costs and litigation risks for rights holders in Europe.
Practice note: Draft contracts with pre-agreed mediation clauses to ensure access to efficient, confidential dispute resolution processes when conflicts arise.
What to monitor: Track the operational rollout of the UPC Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre in 2026 and evolving procedural guidelines from major ADR providers.

news_items.title: Pro = Active? CJEU Asked To Decide
news_items.title_zh: CJEU裁决请求：前药是否可独立获SPC保护？
news_items.title_en: CJEU Asked to Rule on Whether Pro-Drugs Qualify for Independent SPC Protection
news_items.summary: HGF operates a comprehensive network of intellectual property offices spanning major cities across the United Kingdom, Europe, and Ireland.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 爱尔兰律所HGF分析指出，CJEU已受理案件（C-794/25），需裁定已获批活性成分的前药能否独立获得补充保护证书（SPC）。背景涉及武田制药的LDX（右旋安非他命前药）与母药DEX。尽管LDX作为新活性物质获批上市，但法规对前药是否属“同一活性物质”存疑。此裁决将澄清行业争议，解决相关诉讼，明确前药在欧盟的SPC资格标准。
news_items.ai_summary_en: The Court of Justice of the European Union has accepted a referral in case C-794/25, seeking clarification on whether an approved pro-drug of a previously authorized active pharmaceutical ingredient can independently obtain Supplementary Protection Certificate (SPC) protection. The dispute centers on lisdexamfetamine (LDX), a pro-drug marketed by Takeda, and its parent compound dexamphetamine (DEX). Although LDX received marketing authorization as a New Active Substance due to distinct safety and efficacy profiles, questions remain regarding its status under EU regulations. Specifically, it is unclear if pro-drugs constitute the same active substance as their metabolites unless significant differences exist. This ruling aims to resolve ongoing litigation and provide legal certainty for the pharmaceutical industry regarding SPC eligibility criteria for derivative compounds in the European Union.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Rights holders should closely monitor this precedent, as the outcome significantly impacts SPC eligibility for pro-drug pipelines in Europe. If pro-drugs are deemed ineligible for independent protection, companies may face reduced market exclusivity periods. Consequently, stakeholders should evaluate alternative strategies to extend commercial advantages, such as optimizing patent portfolios or leveraging other regulatory exclusivities. It is critical to ensure that submissions regarding safety and efficacy differences robustly meet the threshold for being considered a new active substance. Strengthening the evidentiary record now can help mitigate risks associated with potential legal challenges and ensure compliance with evolving judicial interpretations of EU pharmaceutical regulations.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["CJEU受理前药SPC资格案，需裁定前药能否独立于母药获保护", "争议焦点在于前药是否因安全性或疗效显著不同而视为新活性物质", "裁决结果将消除法律不确定性，直接影响欧盟药品专利策略布局"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["The CJEU must determine if a pro-drug qualifies for independent SPC protection separate from its parent active ingredient.", "The core legal issue involves assessing whether safety or efficacy differences justify treating the pro-drug as a new active substance.", "The final judgment will eliminate legal uncertainty and directly influence pharmaceutical patent strategy within the EU market."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注前药SPC资格判例，评估在欧研发管线的专利延长期限风险", "强化安全与疗效差异证据链，确保满足新活性物质认定标准"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess SPC extension risks for pro-drug development pipelines operating within the European jurisdiction.", "Practice note: Strengthen evidence chains demonstrating significant safety and efficacy differences to support new active substance classification.", "What to monitor: Judicial interpretation of pro-drug status and its impact on future supplementary protection certificate applications."]
core_points_en: The CJEU must determine if a pro-drug qualifies for independent SPC protection separate from its parent active ingredient.
The core legal issue involves assessing whether safety or efficacy differences justify treating the pro-drug as a new active substance.
The final judgment will eliminate legal uncertainty and directly influence pharmaceutical patent strategy within the EU market.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess SPC extension risks for pro-drug development pipelines operating within the European jurisdiction.
Practice note: Strengthen evidence chains demonstrating significant safety and efficacy differences to support new active substance classification.
What to monitor: Judicial interpretation of pro-drug status and its impact on future supplementary protection certificate applications.

news_items.title: LD Düsseldorf, May 12, 2026, Order, UPC_CFI_1747/2025
news_items.title_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫分院裁定：临时禁令需证明单一产品侵权
news_items.title_en: UPC Düsseldorf Dismisses Preliminary Injunction: Strict Single-Product Infringement Proof Required
news_items.summary: The applicant bears the burden of proof for infringement in provisional measures and must demonstrate with sufficient certainty that infringement is more likely than not (Art. 62 UPCA, R. 211 RoP)
news_items.ai_summary_zh: UPC杜塞尔多夫地方法院在2026年5月12日裁决中驳回申请人临时禁令请求。法院强调，申请人须充分证明侵权行为发生的可能性大于不可能性。针对隐形牙套专利，法院认定所有技术特征必须体现在单个产品中，不同阶段的牙套不能组合认定侵权。因申请人仅依赖宣传材料且缺乏具体技术分析，未能满足举证标准，法院未审理有效性等其他抗辩理由。
news_items.ai_summary_en: The UPC Local Division in Düsseldorf dismissed an applicant’s request for preliminary injunctions on May 12, 2026. The Court emphasized that applicants must demonstrate with sufficient certainty that infringement is more likely than not. Regarding clear aligner patents, the ruling established that all technical features of a method claim must be embodied within a single product. Consequently, different treatment phases executed by separate aligners cannot be combined to establish infringement. Since the applicant relied exclusively on promotional materials without providing specific technical analysis or independent assessment of the accused devices, they failed to meet the required standard of proof. As a result, the Court did not examine other defenses, such as validity challenges, highlighting the critical importance of robust technical evidence in provisional proceedings.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国医疗器械企业出口欧洲时，应警惕UPC对‘单一产品’特征的严格解释。在应对临时禁令指控时，需准备详尽的技术比对分析，避免仅依赖营销材料。建议提前进行FTO分析，确保多阶段治疗方案不落入同一专利的单一产品权利要求范围，以规避高额诉讼风险。
news_items.ai_insight_en: This decision underscores the UPC’s strict interpretation of 'single product' requirements for multi-stage medical treatments like clear aligner therapy. Parties seeking or defending against preliminary injunctions must prepare detailed, feature-by-feature technical comparisons rather than relying on marketing collateral. For companies operating in Europe, this highlights the necessity of conducting thorough Freedom-to-Operate analyses early in product development. Ensuring that multi-stage treatment protocols do not inadvertently fall within the scope of a single-product patent claim is essential to mitigate litigation risks and avoid costly enforcement actions at the Unified Patent Court.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["申请人承担临时禁令侵权举证责任，需证明侵权可能性大于不可能性", "方法权利要求的所有特征必须在单个对齐器中实现，不可跨阶段组合", "仅凭宣传材料和推测性证言不足以确立复杂技术领域的侵权事实"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Applicants bear the burden of proof in provisional measures, requiring demonstration that infringement is more likely than not.", "All method claim features must be realized within a single aligner; features across different treatment stages cannot be aggregated.", "Promotional materials and speculative witness statements are insufficient to prove infringement in complex technical fields."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["重视UPC对单一产品特征比对的严格要求，避免跨阶段组合侵权风险", "完善技术证据链，避免仅凭营销材料应对复杂的专利侵权指控"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strict adherence to single-product infringement definitions is critical for multi-stage medical device strategies.", "Practice note: Develop comprehensive technical evidence chains avoiding reliance on general marketing materials for legal defense.", "What to monitor: UPC case law regarding the aggregation of features across distinct treatment phases in method claims."]
core_points_en: Applicants bear the burden of proof in provisional measures, requiring demonstration that infringement is more likely than not.
All method claim features must be realized within a single aligner; features across different treatment stages cannot be aggregated.
Promotional materials and speculative witness statements are insufficient to prove infringement in complex technical fields.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strict adherence to single-product infringement definitions is critical for multi-stage medical device strategies.
Practice note: Develop comprehensive technical evidence chains avoiding reliance on general marketing materials for legal defense.
What to monitor: UPC case law regarding the aggregation of features across distinct treatment phases in method claims.

news_items.title: Taylor Wessing bolsters German patent practice with Eisenführ Speiser hires
news_items.title_zh: Taylor Wessing聘请Eisenführ Speiser合伙人强化德国专利业务
news_items.title_en: Taylor Wessing Strengthens German Patent Practice with Key Hires from Eisenführ Speiser
news_items.summary: The two new partners will join Taylor Wessing on 1 June. The firm is thus underlining its commitment to restoring the renowned patent practice to its former strength following the spin-off of a large team in the autumn. Michael Schneider (56) has more than 20 years of experience in patent litigation, particularly in mobile communications […] The post Taylor Wessing bolsters German patent practice with Eisenführ Speiser hires appeared first on JUVE Patent .
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 国际律所Taylor Wessing宣布将于6月1日吸纳两名来自Eisenführ Speiser的新合伙人，以重建其德国专利实践团队。此举旨在弥补去年秋季大规模团队分拆后的实力损失，恢复该所在德国专利领域的传统优势。新加入的合伙人Michael Schneider拥有逾20年专利诉讼经验，尤其在移动通信领域具备深厚专长。这一人事变动凸显了律所在竞争激烈的德国市场中巩固其知识产权服务地位的战略意图。
news_items.ai_summary_en: International law firm Taylor Wessing has announced the recruitment of two new partners from Eisenführ Speiser, effective June 1. This strategic move aims to rebuild its German patent practice team following the significant departure of a large group last autumn. The hires are intended to restore the firm’s former strength and traditional advantages in the German patent sector. Among the new additions is Michael Schneider, who brings over twenty years of experience in patent litigation, with specialized expertise in mobile communications technologies. This personnel change highlights Taylor Wessing’s commitment to consolidating its intellectual property service position within the highly competitive German market, ensuring robust representation capabilities for clients in complex technology disputes.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国通信企业应关注德国专利诉讼团队变动，特别是具有移动通信背景的资深律师动向。这预示着德国专利诉讼活跃度可能提升，建议提前评估潜在风险，优化在德国市场的IP布局与应诉策略，必要时建立与该类强势律所的合作或预警机制。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should monitor personnel shifts within top-tier German patent firms, particularly those involving senior litigators with deep telecommunications backgrounds. Such moves often signal increased activity and competitiveness in high-stakes patent litigation sectors. Companies operating in Europe should proactively assess potential litigation risks associated with these evolving legal landscapes. It is advisable to optimize intellectual property portfolios and defense strategies accordingly. Establishing early engagement protocols or collaborative frameworks with these prominent legal experts can enhance preparedness for potential enforcement actions or defensive proceedings in the German jurisdiction.

news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Taylor Wessing recruits two new partners on June 1 to significantly strengthen its German patent litigation team capabilities.", "The hiring strategy addresses previous team departures, aiming to restore the firm's leading market position in Germany.", "New partner Michael Schneider offers over twenty years of specialized patent litigation experience in mobile communications."]

news_items.title: Legal holds and document preservation: How to get on the front foot
news_items.title_zh: 法律保留与文件保全：如何掌握诉讼主动权
news_items.title_en: Legal Holds and Document Preservation: Securing Early Procedural Advantage
news_items.summary: Identification and preservation of documentary evidence at the early stages of a dispute isn’t just best practice, it is a mandatory procedural requirem...
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 文章指出，在争议早期识别并保存文件证据不仅是最佳实践，更是强制性程序要求。依据《民事诉讼规则》及PD57AD，当当事人知晓可能成为诉讼方时，必须立即采取具体步骤识别并保存相关文件。这包括实施“停止时钟”的数据保留措施。及时且妥善的法律保留能确保在后续收集和审查阶段前完成初步工作，从而在诉讼中占据有利地位，避免因证据灭失而面临不利后果。
news_items.ai_summary_en: Identifying and preserving documentary evidence at the early stages of a dispute is not merely best practice but a mandatory procedural requirement under English law. Although there is no formal definition of a 'legal hold', parties face strict obligations to take specific, timely steps to identify and preserve documents related to actual or potential litigation. This duty arises under the Civil Procedure Rules and Practice Direction 57AD when a party knows it may become involved in proceedings. Immediate 'stop the clock' measures on data are required to prevent automatic deletion or overwriting. Properly implemented legal holds ensure that initial preservation is completed before collection and review phases begin. This proactive approach helps parties avoid adverse consequences from evidence loss and positions them favorably for subsequent disclosure duties in UK litigation.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对欧洲IP纠纷时，应建立即时响应机制。一旦收到律师函或知悉潜在诉讼，须立即冻结相关电子数据与文档，防止自动覆盖。忽视此程序义务可能导致证据被排除或面临制裁。建议提前制定合规预案，确保在UPC或英国法院诉讼中有效履行披露义务，维护自身合法权益。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Parties facing potential IP disputes in the UK must establish robust internal protocols for immediate document preservation upon receiving notice of potential claims. Failure to implement timely legal holds can result in severe procedural sanctions, including the exclusion of critical evidence. Organizations should integrate these preservation duties into their broader compliance frameworks to ensure readiness for disclosure requirements under the Civil Procedure Rules. Proactive management of electronic data prevents accidental spoliation and supports a defensible position during litigation. This approach is essential for maintaining procedural integrity and protecting substantive rights throughout the dispute resolution process.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法律保留是强制性程序义务，非仅最佳实践，需严格遵守", "触发点为知晓可能成为诉讼当事人，须立即启动文件识别与保存", "需执行“停止时钟”数据保留，确保早期保全以利于后续举证"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Legal holds constitute a mandatory procedural obligation requiring strict compliance rather than serving as optional best practices.", "The preservation trigger occurs upon knowledge of potential litigation, necessitating immediate identification and safeguarding of relevant documents.", "Implementing 'stop the clock' data retention measures ensures early preservation facilitates effective later evidence collection."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立即时响应机制，知悉潜在诉讼即冻结相关电子数据", "重视程序合规，避免因未履行证据保全义务导致证据失效"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish immediate response mechanisms to freeze electronic data upon awareness of potential litigation risks.", "Practice note: Prioritize procedural compliance in evidence preservation to prevent invalidation of key documents due to failure to act.", "What to monitor: Ensure alignment with evolving disclosure standards under Practice Direction 57AD and Civil Procedure Rules."]

news_items.title: Ban on the use of meat names for plant-based alternatives
news_items.title_zh: 欧盟达成禁令：植物基替代品禁用肉类名称
news_items.title_en: EU Agreement Bans Specific Meat Names for Plant-Based Alternatives to Prevent Consumer Misleading
news_items.summary: The EU recently reached an agreement on the question of whether a meat name could be used for plant-based veg products marketed as a substitute for meat.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟机构达成协议，禁止在植物基替代产品上使用特定肉类名称（如鸡肉、牛肉），旨在防止消费者被误导并依据《欧盟商标条例》第7条避免注册误导性商标。通用词汇如“汉堡”仍可使用。该禁令可能导致含肉类名称的植物基品牌无法继续使用现有注册商标，迫使供应商修改包装及品牌策略以符合新规。
news_items.ai_summary_en: The European Parliament, Council, and member states have agreed to prohibit the use of specific meat names on plant-based alternatives. This measure aims to prevent consumer confusion and ensure clarity regarding product nature, aligning with Article 7 of the EU Trademark Regulation (EUTMR), which bars registration of marks likely to mislead the public about quality or origin. While generic terms like 'burger' or 'sausage' remain permissible for describing vegetarian products, specific references to animal parts such as chicken, beef, or bacon are now reserved exclusively for actual meat. Consequently, existing trademarks containing these prohibited terms may face usage restrictions. Suppliers must remove such indications from packaging and reassess their branding strategies to maintain compliance with EU regulations designed to protect consumers from misleading commercial practices.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 对在欧出口植物基食品的中国企业而言，需立即审查现有商标及包装，移除鸡、牛等具体肉类词汇以避免侵权或违规。建议保留burger等通用词，重新设计品牌标识。同时评估现有注册商标的有效性，必要时进行变更或新申请，确保符合欧盟反误导规定，降低法律风险。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Market participants offering plant-based alternatives in the EU must urgently audit their current trademark portfolios and product packaging. Brands utilizing specific meat-related terms risk non-compliance with the new ban, potentially rendering protected marks unusable for their intended goods. Companies should transition to generic descriptors like 'veggie burger' while evaluating the validity of existing registrations. Proactive rebranding and potential new trademark applications are advisable to secure legal protection under the updated regulatory framework. This strategic adjustment minimizes enforcement risks and ensures continued market access without violating EU rules against misleading commercial practices.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["欧盟禁止植物基产品使用特定动物部位名称，以防误导公众", "依据EUTMR第7条，误导性商标不得注册，现有品牌可能受限", "通用描述词如burger允许使用，但需移除具体肉类指代词汇"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Specific animal part names like chicken or beef are banned on plant-based products to prevent misleading consumers about the true nature of the goods.", "Article 7 of the EUTMR prohibits registering trademarks that mislead the public, potentially invalidating existing brands using restricted meat terminology.", "Generic descriptive terms such as burger remain allowed, but specific meat references must be removed from packaging and marketing materials immediately."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["立即审查在欧植物基产品包装，移除具体肉类名称以合规", "评估现有含肉类词商标风险，及时调整品牌策略应对禁令"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Urgent review of EU plant-based product packaging is required to remove specific meat names and ensure regulatory compliance.", "Practice note: Assess the viability of existing trademarks containing meat terms and adjust brand strategies to avoid infringement or invalidation.", "What to monitor: Ongoing enforcement actions by EU authorities regarding the use of prohibited animal-specific terminology in food marketing."]
core_points_en: Specific animal part names like chicken or beef are banned on plant-based products to prevent misleading consumers about the true nature of the goods.
Article 7 of the EUTMR prohibits registering trademarks that mislead the public, potentially invalidating existing brands using restricted meat terminology.
Generic descriptive terms such as burger remain allowed, but specific meat references must be removed from packaging and marketing materials immediately.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Urgent review of EU plant-based product packaging is required to remove specific meat names and ensure regulatory compliance.
Practice note: Assess the viability of existing trademarks containing meat terms and adjust brand strategies to avoid infringement or invalidation.
What to monitor: Ongoing enforcement actions by EU authorities regarding the use of prohibited animal-specific terminology in food marketing.
