news_items.title: Court Of Appeal, April 30, 2026, Order Rejecting Appeal As Inadmissible, UPC_CoA_1/2026
news_items.title_zh: UPC上诉法院裁定驳回上诉：程序合规与费用担保标准
news_items.title_en: UPC Court of Appeal Rejects Adobe Appeal: Strict Procedural Compliance and Cost Security Standards
news_items.summary: The Court of Appeal verifies ex officio compliance with the time limits and rules governing the appeal. When deciding on the admissibility of an appeal, an order rejecting the appeal as...
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）上诉法院在Adobe诉Keeex案中，裁定驳回上诉并确认程序规则需严格适用以保障法律确定性。法院依职权审查上诉期限，认定驳回裁定未超出请求范围。此外，法院重申原告与被告的相对财务状况不构成Rule 158下的费用担保考量因素。该判决强化了UPC程序规则的刚性，强调避免司法任意性，对后续涉及费用担保及程序合规的案件具有先例约束力。
news_items.ai_summary_en: In the case of Adobe Inc. v. Keeex SAS, the Unified Patent Court (UPC) Court of Appeal dismissed the appeal, reinforcing the strict application of procedural rules to ensure legal certainty. The Court conducted an ex officio review of appeal deadlines, confirming that a rejection order for inadmissibility does not exceed the scope of the claims. Furthermore, the judgment reaffirmed established case law stating that the relative financial disparity between claimant and defendant is not a valid criterion under Rule 158 of the Rules of Procedure for determining cost security. This decision underscores the UPC’s commitment to preventing arbitrary judicial treatment and highlights the binding nature of procedural compliance in future litigation involving cost security and admissibility challenges.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，须严守程序期限，避免因技术性违规导致权利丧失。针对费用担保，不能仅凭双方财力差距抗辩，需准备实质性的胜诉可能性证据。建议加强内部IP合规流程，特别是跨境诉讼中的程序管理，以降低因程序失误带来的高额成本风险。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Litigants before the UPC must prioritize rigorous adherence to procedural timelines and formal requirements, as technical non-compliance can lead to immediate dismissal regardless of substantive merits. Regarding cost security, arguments based solely on financial disparity are unlikely to succeed; instead, parties should focus on demonstrating a strong prima facie case regarding the likelihood of success on the merits. Legal teams should strengthen internal cross-border litigation protocols to manage procedural risks effectively. Monitoring how the Court applies these strict standards in subsequent cases will be crucial for developing robust defense strategies and minimizing exposure to high procedural costs.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院依职权严格审查上诉期限与程序规则，确保法律确定性及非歧视原则", "驳回上诉作为不可受理裁定，不视为超出当事人诉讼请求的范围", "当事人相对财务状况不构成申请费用担保的法定依据，维持既往判例立场"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court strictly reviews appeal deadlines and procedural rules ex officio to uphold legal certainty and prevent discriminatory or arbitrary judicial administration.", "An order rejecting an appeal as inadmissible is legally distinct and does not constitute a decision exceeding the scope of the parties' specific claims.", "Relative financial differences between parties do not serve as a statutory basis for cost security adjustments under Rule 158, maintaining consistent prior precedent."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["严守UPC程序期限，规避因技术性违规导致的权利丧失风险", "费用担保抗辩需侧重胜诉可能性，而非单纯依赖财力对比"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure strict adherence to UPC procedural deadlines to avoid rights forfeiture due to technical violations.", "Practice note: Substantiate cost security defenses with evidence of merit rather than relying on comparative financial strength.", "What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 158 and ex officio admissibility checks in UPC cost security proceedings."]
core_points_en: The Court strictly reviews appeal deadlines and procedural rules ex officio to uphold legal certainty and prevent discriminatory or arbitrary judicial administration.
An order rejecting an appeal as inadmissible is legally distinct and does not constitute a decision exceeding the scope of the parties' specific claims.
Relative financial differences between parties do not serve as a statutory basis for cost security adjustments under Rule 158, maintaining consistent prior precedent.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure strict adherence to UPC procedural deadlines to avoid rights forfeiture due to technical violations.
Practice note: Substantiate cost security defenses with evidence of merit rather than relying on comparative financial strength.
What to monitor: Future applications of Rule 158 and ex officio admissibility checks in UPC cost security proceedings.

news_items.title: EPO Enlarged Board Clarifies: Publicly Available Products Are Prior Art, Even If Irreproducible
news_items.title_zh: EPO扩大上诉委员会：公开产品即便不可再现亦属现有技术
news_items.title_en: EPO Enlarged Board: Publicly Available Products Are Prior Art Even If Irreproducible
news_items.summary: The European Patent Office's Enlarged Board has fundamentally altered the landscape of prior art assessment, establishing that products made publicly available before a patent filing date constitute prior art regardless of whether they can be analyzed or reproduced. This landmark decision removes the long-standing reproducibility requirement, making market availability alone sufficient to destroy novelty and significantly raising the stakes for timely patent filings.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 2025年7月2日，EPO扩大上诉委员会在G1/23案中裁定，申请日前公开的产品构成现有技术，无论其成分是否可被分析或再现。该决定推翻了G1/92案确立的“可再现性”要求，认为仅市场可获得性即足以破坏新颖性。此举消除了长期存在的法律不确定性，显著提高了及时提交专利申请的重要性，任何上市前公开均可能使后续专利无效。
news_items.ai_summary_en: On July 2, 2025, the Enlarged Board of Appeal of the European Patent Office issued decision G 1/23, ruling that products made publicly available before a patent application’s filing date constitute prior art under Article 54(2) EPC. This applies regardless of whether their composition or internal structure can be analyzed or reproduced by a skilled person. The decision overturns the long-standing reproducibility requirement established in G 1/92, establishing that market availability alone is sufficient to destroy novelty. This landmark ruling eliminates significant legal uncertainty regarding the status of irreproducible products and substantially raises the stakes for timely patent filings. Consequently, any public disclosure prior to filing, including sales or exhibitions, may invalidate subsequent patent rights, fundamentally altering the landscape of prior art assessment in Europe.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕产品上市前的任何公开行为，包括展会展示或销售，因即使技术细节未完全披露，产品本身也可作为现有技术驳回专利。建议建立严格的保密与申请时间管理机制，确保在申请日前不公开核心技术，避免在新规下丧失新颖性。
news_items.ai_insight_en: This decision fundamentally shifts the risk profile for product launches in Europe. Companies must now treat any public availability of a product as potentially fatal to future patent claims, irrespective of whether competitors can technically replicate the invention. Strict confidentiality protocols are essential before any market exposure. Organizations should prioritize securing European patent applications before engaging in trade shows, sales, or other public disclosures. Failure to align filing timelines with commercial activities may result in irreversible loss of novelty. Monitoring this new standard is critical for global IP strategy, particularly for industries relying on complex chemical or material compositions where analysis was previously considered a barrier to prior art status.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["推翻G1/92先例，取消现有技术认定中的“可再现性”硬性门槛", "确立市场公开即构成现有技术原则，无论内部结构能否被反向工程", "极大扩展现有技术范围，对专利新颖性审查及申请策略产生深远影响"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Overturns G 1/92 precedent by removing the strict reproducibility threshold for determining prior art status.", "Establishes that market availability constitutes prior art regardless of whether internal structures can be reverse-engineered.", "Significantly expands the scope of prior art, profoundly impacting novelty examination and patent application strategies."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["强化申请前保密管理，严防产品上市导致新颖性丧失风险", "调整全球布局策略，优先在欧洲完成申请后再进行市场推广"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Strengthen pre-filing confidentiality management to prevent novelty loss risks from premature product launches.", "Practice note: Adjust global portfolio strategies by prioritizing European patent filings before initiating market promotion or sales.", "What to monitor: How national courts and examiners apply this broader prior art definition to complex, non-reproducible technologies."]
core_points_en: Overturns G 1/92 precedent by removing the strict reproducibility threshold for determining prior art status.
Establishes that market availability constitutes prior art regardless of whether internal structures can be reverse-engineered.
Significantly expands the scope of prior art, profoundly impacting novelty examination and patent application strategies.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Strengthen pre-filing confidentiality management to prevent novelty loss risks from premature product launches.
Practice note: Adjust global portfolio strategies by prioritizing European patent filings before initiating market promotion or sales.
What to monitor: How national courts and examiners apply this broader prior art definition to complex, non-reproducible technologies.

news_items.title: PMAC Advances Operations with New Procedural Rules and Launch of Case Management System
news_items.title_zh: UPC专利调解仲裁中心发布新规并上线案件管理系统
news_items.title_en: UPC Patent Mediation Centre Adopts Rules and Launches Case Management System
news_items.summary: The Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) of the Unified Patent Court (UPC) has taken significant steps toward full operational readiness,
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）行政委员会于2026年4月正式通过专利调解和仲裁中心（PMAC）的程序规则，并于5月12日上线专用案件管理系统（CCMS）。PMAC旨在提供调解、仲裁等替代性争议解决服务，涵盖欧洲专利及标准必要专利（SEP）纠纷。此举标志着PMAC具备全面运营能力，为权利人提供了高效灵活的专利争议解决新途径，进一步完善了UPC生态系统。
news_items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Administrative Committee formally adopted the procedural rules for the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) in April 2026, establishing its legal foundation. Subsequently, on May 12, 2026, PMAC launched its dedicated Case Management System, enabling electronic submission of alternative dispute resolution requests. This development marks a significant step toward full operational readiness for the Centre. PMAC offers mediation, arbitration, and expert determination services covering European patents, Unitary Patents, and Standard Essential Patent disputes. These structured mechanisms provide efficient, flexible options within the UPC ecosystem, aiming to enhance access to effective patent dispute resolution alongside traditional litigation pathways.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧涉及专利纠纷时，可关注UPC-PMAC提供的调解与仲裁渠道。相比传统诉讼，ADR程序更具灵活性与保密性，有助于降低维权成本并加速SEP许可谈判进程。建议企业评估将部分非核心或复杂跨国争议转入PMAC处理，以优化全球知识产权战略布局。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Parties involved in European patent disputes should evaluate the UPC-PMAC as a viable alternative to traditional litigation. The ADR mechanisms offer greater flexibility and confidentiality, which can significantly reduce enforcement costs and accelerate resolution timelines. For Standard Essential Patent licensing negotiations, these channels may facilitate more constructive dialogue and potentially yield favorable settlement terms. Stakeholders are advised to assess whether non-core or complex cross-border conflicts could benefit from PMAC procedures, thereby optimizing global intellectual property strategy and resource allocation.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["UPC行政委员会正式批准PMAC程序规则，奠定法律与运营基础", "PMAC案件管理系统正式上线，支持电子提交替代性争议解决请求", "服务范围涵盖欧洲专利及SEP/FRAND案件，提供多元ADR选项"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["UPC Administrative Committee approved PMAC procedural rules, establishing the legal and operational framework for its activities.", "PMAC Case Management System went live on May 12, supporting electronic submissions for alternative dispute resolution proceedings.", "Service scope covers European patents and SEP/FRAND cases, offering diverse ADR options including mediation and arbitration."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用PMAC的ADR机制可降低在欧专利诉讼成本并提升解决效率", "针对SEP/FRAND纠纷，可探索通过调解仲裁达成更优许可条件"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilizing PMAC ADR mechanisms can lower European patent litigation costs and improve resolution efficiency.", "Practice note: For SEP/FRAND disputes, explore mediation and arbitration to achieve optimized licensing conditions.", "What to monitor: Track the commencement of mediation services in late May 2026 and subsequent arbitration rollout schedules."]
core_points_en: UPC Administrative Committee approved PMAC procedural rules, establishing the legal and operational framework for its activities.
PMAC Case Management System went live on May 12, supporting electronic submissions for alternative dispute resolution proceedings.
Service scope covers European patents and SEP/FRAND cases, offering diverse ADR options including mediation and arbitration.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilizing PMAC ADR mechanisms can lower European patent litigation costs and improve resolution efficiency.
Practice note: For SEP/FRAND disputes, explore mediation and arbitration to achieve optimized licensing conditions.
What to monitor: Track the commencement of mediation services in late May 2026 and subsequent arbitration rollout schedules.

news_items.title: Keeping your word
news_items.title_zh: 保持信誉：文字商标用于Logo仍视为真实使用
news_items.title_en: UKIPO Confirms Registered Word Marks Used in Logos Constitute Genuine Use
news_items.summary: Using a registered word mark as part of a logo mark does not preclude a finding of genuine use – as long as the words remain the dominant element, writes Trecina Sookhoo. O/0080/26, Kilburn & Strode LLP v Easy Limo UK Ltd; O/0028/26, Kilburn & Strode LLP v EasyTerra Besloten Vennootschap; O/1148/25, Kilburn & Strode LLP v Easy Avenues Ltd, UK IPO.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 本文分析英国知识产权局（UKIPO）近期三起案件（O/0080/26等），探讨将注册文字商标作为图形标识一部分使用的法律效力。文章指出，只要文字元素在整体标识中保持主导地位，这种使用方式并不妨碍被认定为“真实使用”。该裁决澄清了商标使用形式的合规性标准，为权利人在品牌视觉升级中保留法律保护提供了依据，强调了维持商标显著特征的重要性。
news_items.ai_summary_en: This analysis examines three recent UK Intellectual Property Office decisions involving Kilburn & Strode LLP against various respondents. The central issue concerns whether incorporating a registered word mark into a composite logo design satisfies the legal requirement for genuine use. The rulings clarify that such usage does not preclude a finding of genuine use, provided the word elements remain the dominant component of the overall visual presentation. This interpretation reinforces the principle that minor aesthetic modifications or integration into broader branding do not invalidate rights if the core distinctive character is preserved. These cases provide critical guidance on compliance standards for trademark maintenance, ensuring that brands can evolve visually while retaining robust legal protection under UK law.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在英欧市场进行品牌视觉升级时，常将文字与图形结合。需注意保留文字的主导地位，避免因过度设计导致被视为新标识而丧失原有保护。建议定期审查商标使用证据，确保符合“真实使用”标准，以防因连续五年不使用而被撤销注册，保障品牌资产安全。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Brands frequently update visual identities by combining text with graphic elements. To maintain valid protection, it is essential that the registered word mark retains its dominant position within the new composite design. Over-designing or obscuring the text may result in the usage being treated as a new, unregistered sign, risking loss of existing rights. Regular audits of trademark use evidence are necessary to demonstrate compliance with genuine use standards. This proactive approach helps prevent cancellation actions based on non-use, thereby safeguarding valuable brand assets and ensuring continuity of legal protection across European markets.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["文字商标融入图形Logo若保持主导元素，可认定构成真实使用", "UKIPO在多起案件中确认非纯粹文字形式的使用符合维持注册要求", "权利人需确保修改后的标识未改变原注册商标的显著特征"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Integrating a registered word mark into a logo constitutes genuine use if the words remain the dominant element.", "The UK IPO confirmed in multiple cases that non-purely textual use meets registration maintenance requirements.", "Rights holders must ensure modified identifiers do not alter the distinctive character of the original registered mark."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["品牌视觉升级应保留文字主导地位以维持注册有效性", "定期审查使用证据以防因未真实使用导致商标被撤销"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Preserve word dominance during visual upgrades to maintain registration validity and avoid rights erosion.", "Practice note: Conduct regular reviews of use evidence to ensure compliance with genuine use standards and prevent revocation.", "What to monitor: Monitor UK IPO decisions regarding composite marks to adapt branding strategies without compromising distinctiveness."]
core_points_en: Integrating a registered word mark into a logo constitutes genuine use if the words remain the dominant element.
The UK IPO confirmed in multiple cases that non-purely textual use meets registration maintenance requirements.
Rights holders must ensure modified identifiers do not alter the distinctive character of the original registered mark.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Preserve word dominance during visual upgrades to maintain registration validity and avoid rights erosion.
Practice note: Conduct regular reviews of use evidence to ensure compliance with genuine use standards and prevent revocation.
What to monitor: Monitor UK IPO decisions regarding composite marks to adapt branding strategies without compromising distinctiveness.

news_items.title: The distinguishing mark of man
news_items.title_zh: 名人之名非版权保护对象：奥威尔遗产诉EUIPO案评析
news_items.title_en: Copyright Protects Works, Not Names: EUIPO Rejects Orwell Estate Trademark Claim
news_items.summary: No matter how famous an author is, copyright exists to protect their work, not their name, writes Matthew Harris. R 2248/2019-G, The Estate of the Late Sonia Brownell Orwell v EUIPO.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 本文评析了EUIPO上诉委员会在R 2248/2019-G案中的决定，涉及已故作家乔治·奥威尔的遗产管理人Sonia Brownell Orwell。文章指出，尽管作者享有盛誉，但版权法的宗旨在于保护其创作的作品，而非其姓名或名声。该案件凸显了在欧盟商标注册中，试图通过版权主张来垄断知名人物姓名的法律局限性，强调了知识产权不同领域间的界限。
news_items.ai_summary_en: This commentary analyzes the EUIPO Board of Appeal decision in case R 2248/2019-G, involving the Estate of the late Sonia Brownell Orwell. The article clarifies that copyright law is designed to protect creative works, not an author's name or public reputation, regardless of their fame. The case highlights the legal limitations of attempting to monopolize a famous person's name through copyright claims in EU trademark registration. It underscores the distinct boundaries between different intellectual property rights, demonstrating that copyright cannot serve as a basis for securing exclusive trademark rights over a celebrity's name.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧布局品牌时，应明确版权与商标权的边界。切勿试图利用版权法垄断通用名称或名人姓名以获取商标独占权，此举在欧盟缺乏法律依据。建议企业建立清晰的IP组合策略，分别通过商标和版权保护不同的资产要素，避免因权利基础混淆导致注册失败或无效风险。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Practitioners must clearly distinguish between the subject matter protected by copyright and trademarks to avoid confusion regarding rights bases. In the EU, attempting to use copyright law to monopolize generic terms or celebrity names for trademark exclusivity lacks legal foundation. Companies should establish clear IP portfolio strategies, protecting different asset elements through appropriate rights. This approach prevents registration failures or invalidation risks arising from mixed rights bases. When registering trademarks containing celebrity elements in Europe, it is crucial to assess the legal feasibility of any accompanying copyright claims.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["版权法旨在保护作品表达，不延伸至保护作者的姓名或公众声誉", "EUIPO驳回奥威尔遗产方基于版权主张注册其姓名的尝试", "知名人物姓名不能仅凭版权理由获得排他性商标保护"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Copyright law protects the expression of works and does not extend to protecting an author's name or public reputation.", "The EUIPO rejected the Orwell Estate's attempt to register the author's name based on copyright grounds.", "Famous names cannot obtain exclusive trademark protection solely by relying on copyright arguments."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["厘清版权与商标保护客体差异，避免权利基础混淆", "在欧注册含名人元素商标需评估版权主张的法律可行性"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Distinguish copyright and trademark protection objects to prevent confusion over rights bases.", "Practice note: Assess the legal feasibility of copyright claims when registering trademarks with celebrity elements in the EU.", "What to monitor: EUIPO decisions regarding the intersection of copyright claims and trademark registration for famous names."]
core_points_en: Copyright law protects the expression of works and does not extend to protecting an author's name or public reputation.
The EUIPO rejected the Orwell Estate's attempt to register the author's name based on copyright grounds.
Famous names cannot obtain exclusive trademark protection solely by relying on copyright arguments.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Distinguish copyright and trademark protection objects to prevent confusion over rights bases.
Practice note: Assess the legal feasibility of copyright claims when registering trademarks with celebrity elements in the EU.
What to monitor: EUIPO decisions regarding the intersection of copyright claims and trademark registration for famous names.

news_items.title: How to file an opposition at the European Patent Office (EPO) | Potter Clarkson
news_items.title_zh: 如何在欧洲专利局提起专利异议
news_items.title_en: Procedural Guide to Filing Oppositions at the European Patent Office Against Granted Patents
news_items.summary: An opposition is a central way for third parties to challenge a granted European patent.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 本文详解在欧洲专利局（EPO）对已授权专利提起异议的程序。异议是第三方挑战专利有效性的核心途径，须在法定期限内提交书面或电子申请并缴费。异议理由严格限于《欧洲专利公约》第100条规定的缺乏可专利性、公开不充分或超范围等特定法律依据。文章强调需通过官方渠道如在线 filing 2.0 提交，且费用支付需即时完成，为从业者提供实操指引。
news_items.ai_summary_en: This article provides a comprehensive overview of the procedural requirements for filing an opposition against a granted European patent at the European Patent Office (EPO). It highlights that opposition serves as a primary administrative mechanism for third parties to challenge patent validity. The text details strict filing protocols, including the necessity of submitting written or electronic notices via official channels such as Online Filing 2.0 within statutory time limits. Crucially, it emphasizes that the opposition fee must be paid simultaneously with the filing. Furthermore, the analysis clarifies that challenges are strictly limited to specific legal grounds under Article 100 EPC, such as lack of patentability, insufficient disclosure, or added subject matter, ensuring practitioners understand the narrow scope of admissible arguments.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧获权后应建立监控机制，防范竞争对手利用异议程序拖延或无效专利。若作为异议方，需精准锁定EPC第100条 grounds，避免理由泛化被驳回。建议熟悉EPO电子提交流程，确保程序合规，以低成本高效解决专利争议，维护市场自由实施空间。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Market participants should implement robust post-grant monitoring systems to detect potential opposition threats from competitors early. For rights holders, understanding these risks allows for proactive preparation and defense strategies. Conversely, entities considering filing an opposition must precisely align their arguments with Article 100 EPC grounds to avoid procedural dismissal due to inadmissibility. Familiarity with EPO electronic filing procedures is essential for compliance and efficiency. This approach enables cost-effective resolution of disputes while safeguarding freedom to operate and maintaining competitive market positions without unnecessary litigation delays.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["异议是挑战已授权欧洲专利的核心行政程序", "必须基于EPC第100条特定法律理由如缺乏创造性", "需通过指定渠道提交并同步缴纳官方异议费"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Opposition is a central administrative procedure allowing third parties to effectively challenge the validity of granted European patents.", "Legal grounds for opposition are strictly confined to Article 100 EPC provisions, including lack of inventive step or added subject matter.", "Filings must use designated EPO electronic or written channels, with the official opposition fee paid immediately upon submission."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["建立专利监控机制防范对手发起异议挑战", "精准锁定EPC第100条理由确保异议程序合规"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Establish continuous patent monitoring mechanisms to anticipate and mitigate risks from competitor-initiated opposition proceedings.", "Practice note: Ensure all opposition arguments strictly adhere to Article 100 EPC grounds to prevent rejection based on inadmissibility or procedural errors.", "What to monitor: Track updates to EPO electronic filing systems and statutory deadlines to maintain full procedural compliance during opposition phases."]
core_points_en: Opposition is a central administrative procedure allowing third parties to effectively challenge the validity of granted European patents.
Legal grounds for opposition are strictly confined to Article 100 EPC provisions, including lack of inventive step or added subject matter.
Filings must use designated EPO electronic or written channels, with the official opposition fee paid immediately upon submission.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Establish continuous patent monitoring mechanisms to anticipate and mitigate risks from competitor-initiated opposition proceedings.
Practice note: Ensure all opposition arguments strictly adhere to Article 100 EPC grounds to prevent rejection based on inadmissibility or procedural errors.
What to monitor: Track updates to EPO electronic filing systems and statutory deadlines to maintain full procedural compliance during opposition phases.

news_items.title: Judgment of the General Court in Case T-24/25 Les Éditions Albert René v EUIPO - Works 11 Michał Lubiński (Obelix)
news_items.title_zh: 普通法院撤销EUIPO决定：武器类“Obelix”商标应无效
news_items.title_en: General Court Annuls EUIPO Decision: Obelix Mark for Weapons Must Be Invalidated
news_items.summary: The General Court nullifies EUIPO’s refusal to invalidate the word mark Obelix for weapons, ammunition and explosives
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧盟普通法院在T-24/25案中撤销EUIPO拒绝宣告“Obelix”武器商标无效的决定。出版商Les Éditions Albert René依据在先OBELIX商标及声誉受损申请无效，EUIPO曾以声誉证据不足为由驳回。法院指出EUIPO分析有误，未综合评估商标声誉及两商标间的联系。法院强调需全局考察在先商标显著性及公众联想，认定存在损害在先商标声誉的风险，支持了权利人的主张。
news_items.ai_summary_en: The General Court of the European Union annulled an EUIPO decision refusing to invalidate the word mark OBELIX for weapons, ammunition, and explosives. Les Éditions Albert René, publisher of the Astérix & Obélix series, sought invalidation based on its earlier EU trade mark OBELIX and alleged damage to that mark’s reputation. The EUIPO had previously rejected the application, ruling that proof of the earlier mark’s reputation was insufficient. The General Court found the EUIPO’s analysis incomplete and erroneous, noting it failed to properly consider evidence of use with the ® symbol and in combination with the Asterix sign. The Court emphasized that reputation must be assessed globally, considering all relevant factors. It concluded that the public could associate the marks, creating a risk of unfair advantage or detriment to the distinctive character or repute of the earlier mark, thereby supporting the rights holder’s claim for invalidation.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类裁判口径给出配套安排或更明确口径。
news_items.ai_insight_en: This judgment reinforces the protection of well-known cultural signs against cross-class registration, particularly when distinctiveness and reputation are established through consistent use. Rights holders should maintain robust global monitoring systems to detect potentially infringing applications early. Crucially, parties must accumulate comprehensive evidence of trademark use, including co-branding instances and registered status indicators, to substantiate reputation claims. In opposition or invalidation proceedings, demonstrating the likelihood of public association remains vital, even across disparate product categories, to successfully argue against unfair exploitation of a mark’s goodwill.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院认定EUIPO对在先商标声誉评估不完整，忽视关键使用证据", "强调需全局评估商标间联系，不能仅因商品差异大而否定关联", "撤销EUIPO决定，支持基于声誉受损宣告争议商标无效的诉求"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Court ruled the EUIPO’s assessment of the earlier mark’s reputation was incomplete, ignoring key evidence of commercial use and registration symbols.", "Global assessment is required for trademark links; significant differences in goods alone do not preclude public association or reputational damage.", "The General Court annulled the refusal, upholding the invalidation request based on the risk of detriment to the reputation of the well-known earlier mark."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["避免注册知名文化符号，防范跨类声誉侵权法律风险", "重视商标使用证据留存，积极应对欧盟商标无效宣告挑战"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Well-known brands face lower thresholds for proving cross-class confusion when reputation is clearly evidenced by market presence.", "Practice note: Preserve detailed records of trademark usage, including ® markings and joint branding, to substantiate acquired distinctiveness and reputation.", "What to monitor: Judicial trends in assessing 'link' between marks across non-competing goods, especially involving iconic cultural characters."]
core_points_en: The Court ruled the EUIPO’s assessment of the earlier mark’s reputation was incomplete, ignoring key evidence of commercial use and registration symbols.
Global assessment is required for trademark links; significant differences in goods alone do not preclude public association or reputational damage.
The General Court annulled the refusal, upholding the invalidation request based on the risk of detriment to the reputation of the well-known earlier mark.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Well-known brands face lower thresholds for proving cross-class confusion when reputation is clearly evidenced by market presence.
Practice note: Preserve detailed records of trademark usage, including ® markings and joint branding, to substantiate acquired distinctiveness and reputation.
What to monitor: Judicial trends in assessing 'link' between marks across non-competing goods, especially involving iconic cultural characters.

news_items.title: Creating ongoing awareness among entrepreneurs about the importance of IP
news_items.title_zh: BOIP持续向企业家普及知识产权重要性
news_items.title_en: BOIP Launches New Campaign to Boost Entrepreneur IP Awareness in Benelux
news_items.summary: Unfortunately, many entrepreneurs are still unfamiliar with intellectual property (IP).
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 比荷卢知识产权局（BOIP）指出许多企业家对知识产权（IP）缺乏了解或认为其复杂昂贵。通过“Create, Protect, Benefit”平台及合作伙伴，BOIP旨在展示IP在加强商业地位、促进合作及加速融资方面的优势。该计划已运行六年，2025年营销活动覆盖超百万人次。新campaign旨在持续提升广大企业家对IP的认知，推动IP成为比荷卢地区商业计划的组成部分。
news_items.ai_summary_en: The Benelux Office for Intellectual Property (BOIP) highlights that many entrepreneurs remain unfamiliar with intellectual property or perceive it as complex and costly. Through the 'Create, Protect, Benefit' platform, BOIP and its partners aim to demonstrate IP's strategic value in strengthening business positions, facilitating collaborations, and accelerating access to financing. This initiative has operated for six years across the Netherlands and Belgium, involving partners such as FPS Economy, the Netherlands Patent Office, VLAIO, and Wallonie Entreprendre. In 2025, marketing efforts generated over one million impressions, significantly expanding reach. A new campaign launches this month to continuously raise awareness among a broad entrepreneur base. The ultimate goal, aligned with the Strategic Plan BOIP 2030, is to make IP an integral component of every business plan within the Benelux region.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 在欧中国企业应重视比荷卢地区的IP基础认知建设，利用官方免费资源降低合规成本。建议主动参与此类意识提升活动，将IP纳入早期商业规划，以优化融资环境并增强本地化合作的信任度，避免因地方法规认知不足导致的市场准入障碍。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Market participants operating in the Benelux should leverage official BOIP resources to mitigate compliance costs and enhance local market integration. Integrating IP considerations into early-stage business planning can optimize financing opportunities and strengthen trust in localized collaborations. Companies are advised to actively engage with these awareness initiatives to prevent market entry barriers caused by insufficient understanding of regional regulations. Utilizing free official guides and success stories can provide accessible pathways to effective IP protection, supporting sustainable growth and innovation strategies within the European framework.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["BOIP联合多方伙伴推广IP价值，消除企业家对IP复杂昂贵的误解", "强调IP在强化商业地位、促进合作及获取融资中的关键作用", "新营销战役旨在扩大覆盖面，持续提升比荷卢地区企业家IP意识"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["BOIP collaborates with multiple partners to promote IP value and dispel misconceptions regarding complexity and high costs.", "The initiative emphasizes IP's critical role in reinforcing commercial standing, enabling partnerships, and securing funding.", "A new marketing campaign aims to expand coverage and sustainably elevate IP awareness among Benelux entrepreneurs."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用BOIP官方资源降低IP合规成本，优化比荷卢市场融资环境", "将IP纳入早期商业规划，增强本地化合作信任度与市场准入能力"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage BOIP official resources to reduce IP compliance costs and optimize financing environments in the Benelux market.", "Practice note: Integrate IP strategy into early business planning to enhance trust in local collaborations and improve market access capabilities.", "What to monitor: Ongoing BOIP marketing campaigns and updated practical guides on IP protection for entrepreneurs in the region."]
core_points_en: BOIP collaborates with multiple partners to promote IP value and dispel misconceptions regarding complexity and high costs.
The initiative emphasizes IP's critical role in reinforcing commercial standing, enabling partnerships, and securing funding.
A new marketing campaign aims to expand coverage and sustainably elevate IP awareness among Benelux entrepreneurs.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage BOIP official resources to reduce IP compliance costs and optimize financing environments in the Benelux market.
Practice note: Integrate IP strategy into early business planning to enhance trust in local collaborations and improve market access capabilities.
What to monitor: Ongoing BOIP marketing campaigns and updated practical guides on IP protection for entrepreneurs in the region.

news_items.title: Central Division Paris accepts revocation action against expired patent
news_items.title_zh: UPC巴黎分部：过期专利撤销之诉若具法律利益仍属可受理
news_items.title_en: UPC Paris: Revocation Action Admissible for Expired Patent if Legal Interest Persists
news_items.summary: Huntsman (Europe) BV and Huntsman Holland BV v BASF SE — Central Division Paris, 22 April 2026 (UPC_CFI_461/2025))
news_items.ai_summary_zh: UPC巴黎中央分部在Huntsman诉BASF案中裁定，即使涉案专利已过期，只要原告因追索过往侵权损害赔偿而保留合法法律利益，撤销诉讼即具可受理性。法院重申在创造性评估中不受专利自身技术问题表述限制，可独立界定客观技术问题。此外，辅助请求的审查仅限于针对修改后权利要求的具体无效攻击，不自动沿用对授权权利要求的异议。
news_items.ai_summary_en: In Huntsman v BASF, the UPC Central Division Paris ruled that a revocation action remains admissible even after patent expiry, provided the claimant retains a legitimate legal interest, such as pursuing damages for past infringement. The Court confirmed it is not bound by the objective technical problem stated in the patent specification and may independently define this problem during inventive-step analysis. Furthermore, regarding auxiliary requests, the Court established that only specific invalidity attacks directed at amended claims are considered. Objections raised against granted claims do not automatically carry over to modified versions, requiring parties to precisely target their arguments in amendment proceedings.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业需警惕UPC程序灵活性，即便专利过期，对手仍可借撤销之诉清除障碍以索赔。应对时须精准论证法律利益缺失，并在答复辅助请求时聚焦具体修改点，避免异议范围不当扩大，强化程序抗辩策略。
news_items.ai_insight_en: This decision highlights the procedural flexibility of the UPC, where expired patents can still be challenged to clear obstacles for subsequent damages claims. Parties must rigorously assess whether a plaintiff retains a legitimate legal interest to block such actions early. In response strategies, defendants should carefully tailor their defenses for auxiliary requests, focusing strictly on the specific amendments made. This prevents the unintended expansion of invalidity grounds and ensures that objections to original claims do not automatically prejudice modified versions, thereby strengthening procedural defense positions.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["过期专利撤销案若为追索过往侵权赔偿，因具备法律利益仍可被受理", "法院在创造性分析中有权独立界定客观技术问题，不受专利文本束缚", "辅助请求审查限于针对修改权利要求的具体无效攻击，不自动继承异议"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Revocation actions remain admissible post-expiry if the claimant pursues damages for alleged past infringement.", "Courts may independently determine the objective technical problem and are not confined to the wording used in the patent specification.", "Review of auxiliary requests is limited to specific invalidity attacks on amended claims, without automatic inheritance of prior objections."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕过期专利撤销风险，积极抗辩原告缺乏合法法律利益以阻断诉讼", "优化辅助请求答复策略，严格限定审查范围，防止无效理由不当扩展"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess whether plaintiffs retain legal interest via pending damages claims to challenge admissibility of post-expiry revocation actions.", "Practice note: Tailor responses to auxiliary requests by addressing only specific amendments, preventing automatic extension of objections from granted claims.", "What to monitor: How UPC divisions apply independent definitions of the objective technical problem in future inventive-step assessments."]
core_points_en: Revocation actions remain admissible post-expiry if the claimant pursues damages for alleged past infringement.
Courts may independently determine the objective technical problem and are not confined to the wording used in the patent specification.
Review of auxiliary requests is limited to specific invalidity attacks on amended claims, without automatic inheritance of prior objections.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess whether plaintiffs retain legal interest via pending damages claims to challenge admissibility of post-expiry revocation actions.
Practice note: Tailor responses to auxiliary requests by addressing only specific amendments, preventing automatic extension of objections from granted claims.
What to monitor: How UPC divisions apply independent definitions of the objective technical problem in future inventive-step assessments.

news_items.title: Mind the SEP: UKIPO signals upcoming SEP reform
news_items.title_zh: 英专局信号：即将推进标准必要专利改革
news_items.title_en: UKIPO Signals Upcoming Standard Essential Patent Regulatory Reforms
news_items.summary: On 11 May 2026, the UK Intellectual Property Office (UKIPO) published its Corporate Plan for 2026–2027, outlining its policy priorities for the coming…
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月11日，英国知识产权局（UKIPO）发布2026-2027企业计划，明确将标准必要专利（SEP）作为政策重点。针对当前生态系统存在的许可不透明、必要性不确定及争议解决成本高企等结构性挑战，UKIPO计划在2026年完成政策制定并推动监管框架变革。此举旨在提升英国创新吸引力，虽无即时立法，但预示未来将在许可透明度、SEP必要性认定及纠纷效率方面进行改革。
news_items.ai_summary_en: On 11 May 2026, the UK Intellectual Property Office published its Corporate Plan for 2026–2027, identifying standard essential patents as a key policy priority. The plan acknowledges longstanding structural challenges within the current ecosystem, specifically limited licensing transparency, uncertainty regarding patent essentiality, and costly dispute resolution mechanisms that disproportionately impact small and medium enterprises. While no immediate legislative changes are introduced, the UKIPO intends to complete policy development in 2026 to drive regulatory framework reforms. These initiatives aim to enhance the UK’s attractiveness as a leading jurisdiction for innovation by addressing inefficiencies and improving certainty for stakeholders involved in SEP licensing and enforcement.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国通信及科技企业在英面临更严格的SEP合规环境。建议密切关注UKIPO后续政策细则，提前评估现有许可协议的透明度与必要性条款风险。应加强内部SEP管理能力，积极参与行业咨询，以应对潜在的监管变化及更高的争议解决效率要求，避免在新一轮改革中处于被动地位。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Stakeholders engaged in SEP licensing and enforcement should anticipate a stricter compliance environment driven by upcoming UK regulatory adjustments. It is prudent to evaluate existing licensing agreements for transparency and essentiality clause risks ahead of formal rule changes. Organizations should strengthen internal SEP management capabilities and actively participate in industry consultations to shape emerging standards. Proactive engagement will help mitigate potential liabilities and ensure readiness for more efficient, yet rigorous, dispute resolution processes expected under the new framework.

news_items.title: PMAC's Case Management System now live
news_items.title_zh: PMAC案件管理系统正式上线
news_items.title_en: UPC Patent Mediation Centre Launches Online Case Management System for ADR Proceedings
news_items.summary: As of today, 12 May 2026, the Case Management System of the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre (PMAC) is live
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月12日，统一专利法院（UPC）宣布其专利调解与仲裁中心（PMAC）的案件管理系统（CCMS）正式启用。该系统支持用户在线提交替代性争议解决（ADR）信息及调解程序请求。PMAC网站已设立专门页面提供系统使用指南。此举标志着UPC在优化专利纠纷非诉讼解决机制方面迈出重要一步，为当事人提供了更便捷的调解与仲裁服务渠道，有助于提高争议解决效率并降低诉讼成本。
news_items.ai_summary_en: On 12 May 2026, the Unified Patent Court announced that the Case Management System of its Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre is now fully operational. This digital platform enables parties to submit requests for Alternative Dispute Resolution information sessions and mediation proceedings directly online. The launch represents a significant step in optimizing non-litigious mechanisms for resolving patent disputes within the UPC framework. By providing a dedicated interface for these services, the Centre aims to offer rights holders a more accessible and efficient channel for settlement. This development is expected to enhance the overall efficiency of dispute resolution while helping participants manage and potentially reduce the high costs associated with traditional cross-border patent litigation.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧遭遇专利纠纷时，应关注并利用PMAC的调解与仲裁机制。相比UPC诉讼，ADR程序通常更具成本效益且保密性强。建议企业熟悉CCMS操作流程，在争议初期评估通过调解快速和解的可能性，以规避高昂的跨国诉讼风险并维护商业关系。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Market participants facing patent conflicts in Europe should evaluate the Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre’s services as a strategic alternative to full litigation. ADR procedures generally offer greater cost efficiency and confidentiality compared to standard UPC court proceedings. Rights holders are advised to familiarize themselves with the new Case Management System to facilitate rapid initiation of these processes. Assessing the potential for early settlement through mediation can help mitigate the financial risks of prolonged litigation and preserve valuable commercial relationships during disputes.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["PMAC案件管理系统于2026年5月12日正式上线运行", "支持在线提交ADR信息会话及调解程序相关请求", "官网发布专用页面提供系统操作实务指导"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["The Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre officially launched its Case Management System on 12 May 2026.", "The new platform supports the online submission of requests for ADR information sessions and mediation proceedings.", "A dedicated webpage provides practical guidance and instructions for using the system effectively."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用PMAC调解机制可降低在欧专利维权成本", "熟悉CCMS系统以便高效启动替代性争议解决程序"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Utilizing PMAC mediation mechanisms can significantly lower the costs of enforcing or defending patent rights in Europe.", "Practice note: Familiarity with the CCMS interface is essential for efficiently initiating Alternative Dispute Resolution procedures.", "What to monitor: Monitor updates regarding the adoption rates and procedural outcomes of PMAC-mediated cases."]
core_points_en: The Patent Mediation and Arbitration Centre officially launched its Case Management System on 12 May 2026.
The new platform supports the online submission of requests for ADR information sessions and mediation proceedings.
A dedicated webpage provides practical guidance and instructions for using the system effectively.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Utilizing PMAC mediation mechanisms can significantly lower the costs of enforcing or defending patent rights in Europe.
Practice note: Familiarity with the CCMS interface is essential for efficiently initiating Alternative Dispute Resolution procedures.
What to monitor: Monitor updates regarding the adoption rates and procedural outcomes of PMAC-mediated cases.

news_items.title: Judgment of the Court in Case C-797/23 Meta Platforms Ireland (Fair compensation)
news_items.title_zh: CJEU：成员国可规定新闻出版者获在线使用公平报酬
news_items.title_en: CJEU: Member States May Mandate Fair Remuneration for Online Press Use
news_items.summary: Member States may make provision for publishers of press publications to be entitled to fair remuneration when they grant online service providers authorisation to use those publications
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 欧洲法院在Meta诉意大利AGCOM案中裁定，成员国可立法赋予新闻出版者在授权在线服务提供者使用时获得公平报酬的权利。该权利符合欧盟法律，前提是报酬构成授权对价，且出版者可拒绝授权或免费授权。法院认为，要求平台谈判并提供数据虽限制经营自由，但有助于实现版权市场公平及保障出版业投资回收，平衡了各方权益。
news_items.ai_summary_en: In Case C-797/23 Meta Platforms Ireland, the Court of Justice ruled that EU law permits Member States to legislate fair remuneration for press publishers when authorizing online service providers to use their content. The Court held this right is consistent with the Digital Single Market framework, provided the payment constitutes consideration for authorization and publishers retain the right to refuse licensing or grant it free of charge. While obligations requiring platforms to negotiate and provide data restrict freedom to conduct a business, they are justified by objectives ensuring a fair copyright marketplace and enabling publishers to recoup investments. This balance protects intellectual property and media pluralism while accommodating digital market realities.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国出海媒体及聚合平台需关注欧盟各成员国的国内法转化，特别是意大利等先行国家。应建立合规机制，评估是否触发“公平报酬”义务，避免侵权风险。同时，利用出版者可免费授权的条款，积极争取低成本内容合作，优化内容供应链成本结构。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Online service providers and aggregators operating within the EU should closely monitor how Member States transpose these principles into national law, particularly in jurisdictions like Italy where such regimes are already active. Entities must assess whether their content usage triggers fair compensation obligations to mitigate infringement risks. Furthermore, leveraging the provision allowing free-of-charge authorization offers strategic opportunities for securing low-cost content partnerships. Optimizing supply chain costs through negotiated free licenses can enhance competitive positioning while maintaining compliance with evolving European copyright standards.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["成员国可立法规定出版者就在线使用获公平报酬，符合欧盟法框架", "报酬须为授权对价，出版者有权拒绝授权或提供免费授权", "强制谈判义务旨在平衡经营自由与版权保护，支持出版业生存"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Member States may establish legal frameworks granting press publishers entitlement to fair remuneration for online use of their publications.", "Remuneration must serve as consideration for authorization, with publishers retaining rights to refuse licenses or offer them gratis.", "Mandatory negotiation duties balance business freedom against copyright protection, supporting publisher investment recovery."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注成员国国内法转化，评估在线内容使用的报酬合规义务", "利用免费授权条款，争取低成本内容合作以优化供应链成本"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Assess exposure to fair compensation mandates in key EU markets to manage liability and budget planning.", "Practice note: Utilize free authorization clauses to negotiate cost-effective content licensing agreements with publishers.", "What to monitor: National legislative implementations across Member States regarding mandatory negotiation and data disclosure duties."]
core_points_en: Member States may establish legal frameworks granting press publishers entitlement to fair remuneration for online use of their publications.
Remuneration must serve as consideration for authorization, with publishers retaining rights to refuse licenses or offer them gratis.
Mandatory negotiation duties balance business freedom against copyright protection, supporting publisher investment recovery.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Assess exposure to fair compensation mandates in key EU markets to manage liability and budget planning.
Practice note: Utilize free authorization clauses to negotiate cost-effective content licensing agreements with publishers.
What to monitor: National legislative implementations across Member States regarding mandatory negotiation and data disclosure duties.

news_items.title: La porcelaine de Limoges
news_items.title_zh: 利摩日瓷器获欧盟首项手工艺地理标志保护
news_items.title_en: Limoges Porcelain Becomes First EU Industrial and Artisanal Geographical Indication
news_items.summary: La porcelaine de Limoges sgurtler@inpi.fr 11 mai 2026 Avec l'entrée en vigueur du règlement européen sur les indications géographiques pour les produits artisanaux et industriels le 1er décembre 2025, la porcelaine de Limoges devient ce 15 mai la première indication géographique protégée (IGP) française artisanale et industrielle à être enregistrée au niveau européen auprès de l'Office de l'Union européenne pour la propriété intellectuelle (EUIPO). Cette reconnaissance marque une étape décisive
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 2026年5月15日，利摩日瓷器成为欧盟《手工业和工业产品地理标志条例》生效后，首个在欧盟知识产权局（EUIPO）注册的法国手工艺及工业地理标志（IGP）。该认证基于其自2017年起获得的法国国家地理标志地位，通过简化程序转化而来。此举赋予其在欧盟27国的广泛保护及官方标识使用权，标志着法国传统工艺知识产权保护的重要里程碑，并为其他法国手工艺品的欧盟注册开辟路径。
news_items.ai_summary_en: On May 15, 2026, Limoges porcelain was registered as the first French artisanal and industrial geographical indication at the European Union Intellectual Property Office. This registration follows the entry into force of the new EU regulation on geographical indications for craft and industrial products in December 2025. Leveraging its existing national status granted by INPI in 2017, the designation underwent a simplified transformation process without an opposition phase. The decision grants comprehensive protection across all twenty-seven EU member states and authorizes the use of the official protected geographical indication logo. This milestone underscores the expanded scope of EU IP protection for traditional crafts and sets a significant precedent for other French artisanal sectors seeking similar recognition under the new regulatory framework.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，欧洲层面后续是否会围绕这类官方动向给出配套安排或更明确口径。
news_items.ai_insight_en: The expansion of EU geographical indication protection to include artisanal and industrial goods creates new compliance obligations for global manufacturers. Rights holders exporting ceramics or similar handcrafted items must verify that their labeling does not infringe upon newly protected regional names or specific production methods. Conversely, this regulatory shift offers strategic opportunities for non-EU producers with distinct regional specialties. By navigating the application process for EU geographical indications, these entities can enhance brand equity, secure market differentiation, and strengthen their competitive position within the European single market against imitation products.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["利摩日瓷器成欧盟新规下首个注册的手工艺地理标志", "凭借法国国家GI基础，经简化程序直接转化为欧盟IGP", "获欧盟全境保护权及官方标识使用权，强化品牌壁垒"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Limoges porcelain is the first craft and industrial product to secure EU geographical indication status under the new regulatory framework.", "The designation utilized a simplified transformation procedure based on prior national recognition, bypassing standard EU opposition phases.", "Registration provides uniform legal protection across all twenty-seven EU member states and permits use of the official geographical indication logo."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["警惕欧盟GI新规对陶瓷等手工艺品出口的合规冲击", "推动中国特色产品申请欧盟GI以提升国际品牌溢价"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Exporters of ceramics and artisanal goods must audit product descriptions to avoid infringing on newly protected EU geographical indications.", "Practice note: Rights holders with unique regional characteristics should evaluate eligibility for EU geographical indication registration to secure brand premium and legal exclus…", "What to monitor: Future registrations under the new craft and industrial GI regulation may expand the list of protected terms requiring compliance checks."]
core_points_en: Limoges porcelain is the first craft and industrial product to secure EU geographical indication status under the new regulatory framework.
The designation utilized a simplified transformation procedure based on prior national recognition, bypassing standard EU opposition phases.
Registration provides uniform legal protection across all twenty-seven EU member states and permits use of the official geographical indication logo.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Exporters of ceramics and artisanal goods must audit product descriptions to avoid infringing on newly protected EU geographical indications.
Practice note: Rights holders with unique regional characteristics should evaluate eligibility for EU geographical indication registration to secure brand premium and legal exclus…
What to monitor: Future registrations under the new craft and industrial GI regulation may expand the list of protected terms requiring compliance checks.

news_items.title: UPC: Late filed physical evidence cannot clarify the content of an earlier filed brochure
news_items.title_zh: UPC裁定：迟交实物证据不得用于澄清先前提交的宣传册内容
news_items.title_en: UPC Vienna Division Excludes Late Physical Evidence Clarifying Prior Art Brochure Disclosure
news_items.summary: In a recent decision from the Vienna Local Division of the Unified Patent Court (UPC), the Court maintained EP 3705415, taking a strict...
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）维也纳地方法院在Messerle诉Sabert案中维持涉案专利有效。被告Sabert试图通过迟交实物样品来澄清商业宣传册中产品特征以攻击专利新颖性。法院裁定宣传册为独立现有技术，实物证据不能用于补充或重构其披露内容，以防事后诸葛亮式解读。因该证据实质引入新的无效理由且提交过晚，法院拒绝采纳，强调证据必须基于现有论点发展而非引入新攻击。
news_items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Vienna Local Division maintained European Patent EP 3705415 in infringement proceedings between Messerle and Sabert. The defendant, Sabert, challenged the patent's novelty based on a commercial brochure but submitted physical product samples late to clarify features not clearly discernible in the brochure's two-dimensional images. The Court rejected this evidence, ruling that the brochure constitutes standalone prior art evaluated by the skilled person without external supplementation. Allowing physical evidence would risk reconstructing the disclosure with hindsight. Furthermore, the Court determined that the late submission effectively introduced a new line of attack based on alleged public prior use rather than developing existing arguments regarding the document's content. Consequently, the evidence was inadmissible, and the patent was upheld as the brochure alone did not unambiguously disclose all claimed features.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在UPC诉讼中需严格遵循举证时限与策略一致性。针对新颖性挑战，应确保书面证据本身清晰完整，避免依赖后续实物补强。若主张公开使用，须早期明确提出并单独举证，防止因混淆证据性质导致关键证据被排除，从而丧失无效机会。
news_items.ai_insight_en: This decision underscores the UPC's rigorous approach to procedural discipline and the admissibility of late-filed evidence. Parties must ensure that documentary prior art stands on its own merits without relying on subsequent physical exhibits to interpret ambiguous features. Attempting to bridge gaps in written disclosures using physical evidence risks being characterized as an impermissible attempt to introduce a new attack, such as public use, at a late stage. Litigants should carefully distinguish between clarifying a document's inherent disclosure and asserting separate grounds for invalidity. Early identification of the specific legal basis for each piece of evidence is critical to avoid procedural exclusion and preserve the validity of the challenge.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["法院拒绝采纳迟交的实物证据，因其旨在澄清而非补充先前提交的宣传册披露内容", "宣传册作为独立现有技术，其披露范围由本领域技术人员直接明确认定，禁止事后重构", "迟交证据若实质引入全新无效攻击路径（如公开使用），将因程序不当被排除"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Late-filed physical samples were excluded because they aimed to clarify, rather than supplement, the disclosure of an earlier filed commercial brochure.", "A commercial brochure is treated as independent prior art; its scope is defined by what the skilled person directly derives, prohibiting hindsight reconstruction via external obje…", "Evidence submitted late that introduces a fundamentally new invalidity ground, such as public prior use, is procedurally inadmissible if it does not develop existing arguments."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["UPC对迟交证据持严格态度，企业需确保书面证据自身具备充分清晰度", "区分不同无效理由的证据提交策略，避免混合导致程序性驳回"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Ensure documentary prior art is self-sufficient; do not rely on late physical exhibits to interpret ambiguous visual disclosures in novelty attacks.", "Practice note: Clearly distinguish between arguments based on document disclosure and those based on public use to prevent late submissions from being deemed inadmissible new atta…", "What to monitor: UPC divisions' consistent application of strict admissibility rules regarding late evidence that attempts to reconstruct prior art disclosures with hindsight."]
core_points_en: Late-filed physical samples were excluded because they aimed to clarify, rather than supplement, the disclosure of an earlier filed commercial brochure.
A commercial brochure is treated as independent prior art; its scope is defined by what the skilled person directly derives, prohibiting hindsight reconstruction via external obje…
Evidence submitted late that introduces a fundamentally new invalidity ground, such as public prior use, is procedurally inadmissible if it does not develop existing arguments.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Ensure documentary prior art is self-sufficient; do not rely on late physical exhibits to interpret ambiguous visual disclosures in novelty attacks.
Practice note: Clearly distinguish between arguments based on document disclosure and those based on public use to prevent late submissions from being deemed inadmissible new atta…
What to monitor: UPC divisions' consistent application of strict admissibility rules regarding late evidence that attempts to reconstruct prior art disclosures with hindsight.

news_items.title: Of a Dame and the King (of Beer): when an indefinite trade mark licence can end | Potter Clarkson
news_items.title_zh: 无限期商标许可非永久：英国法院裁定可凭合理通知终止
news_items.title_en: UK Courts Rule Indefinite Trademark Licences Are Not Perpetual and Can Be Terminated on Reasonable Notice
news_items.summary: Of a Dame and the King (of Beer) – An indefinite trade mark licence doesn’t necessarily last forever and the termination of a long-running commercial arrangement doesn’t necessarily require a long period of notice to be “reasonable”.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 英国上诉法院在Zaha Hadid案中裁定，“无限期”商标许可不等于“永久”，允许被许可方在给予合理通知后终止合同。随后枢密院在Budweiser案中进一步指出，合理通知期的长短取决于商业背景而非仅看合作时长。该系列判决澄清了长期商业安排中默示终止权的存在，强调合同解释需符合商业逻辑，打破了无限期许可即不可解除的传统认知。
news_items.ai_summary_en: Recent decisions by the UK Court of Appeal and the Privy Council clarify that an 'indefinite' trademark licence does not equate to a perpetual grant. In the Zaha Hadid case, the Court of Appeal determined that such licences contain an implied right for either party to terminate upon providing reasonable notice. This principle was further refined in the Budweiser distribution dispute, where the Privy Council emphasized that determining what constitutes 'reasonable' notice depends heavily on the specific commercial context rather than merely the duration of the business relationship. These rulings dismantle the traditional assumption that indefinite licences are irrevocable, establishing that long-running commercial arrangements do not automatically require extended notice periods for termination to be considered lawful. The judgments underscore the necessity of interpreting contract terms through a commercially logical lens.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在欧签署长期或无限期商标许可时，切勿默认其不可撤销。建议在合同中明确约定终止条款、通知期限及违约责任，以规避法律不确定性。若面临纠纷，应重点举证商业背景的合理性，利用判例主张默示终止权，避免陷入被动锁定的商业困境。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Parties entering into long-term or indefinite trademark licensing arrangements in Europe should not assume these rights are irrevocable or immune from termination. Legal uncertainty arises when contracts lack explicit exit mechanisms. To mitigate risk, licensors and licensees must draft clear termination clauses, define specific notice periods, and outline liability for breach. In potential disputes, focus on evidencing the commercial rationale behind the proposed notice period. Leveraging recent precedents allows parties to argue for implied termination rights based on business logic, preventing involuntary lock-in and ensuring flexibility in evolving market conditions.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["无限期商标许可不等同于永久许可，隐含双方无意永久绑定", "被许可方可依据默示权利，通过给予合理通知终止许可协议", "合理通知期的判定核心在于具体商业背景，而非单纯的合作年限"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["An indefinite trademark licence is legally distinct from a perpetual licence, implying no intention for permanent binding obligations between the parties.", "Licensees possess an implied right to terminate indefinite agreements by providing reasonable notice, even without express contractual termination clauses.", "The determination of reasonable notice relies primarily on the specific commercial context and operational realities, not solely on the length of the partnership."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["签约时需明确终止机制，避免依赖模糊的无限期条款", "争议中应侧重论证商业背景以确立合理通知期的合法性"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Avoid relying on ambiguous 'indefinite' clauses; explicitly define termination mechanisms and notice periods during contract negotiation to prevent future disput…", "Practice note: In litigation, prioritize demonstrating the commercial context to establish the legality of the notice period, utilizing recent case law to support claims of implie…", "What to monitor: Watch for further judicial interpretations regarding what constitutes 'reasonable' notice in diverse commercial sectors to refine licensing strategies and risk as…"]
core_points_en: An indefinite trademark licence is legally distinct from a perpetual licence, implying no intention for permanent binding obligations between the parties.
Licensees possess an implied right to terminate indefinite agreements by providing reasonable notice, even without express contractual termination clauses.
The determination of reasonable notice relies primarily on the specific commercial context and operational realities, not solely on the length of the partnership.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Avoid relying on ambiguous 'indefinite' clauses; explicitly define termination mechanisms and notice periods during contract negotiation to prevent future disput…
Practice note: In litigation, prioritize demonstrating the commercial context to establish the legality of the notice period, utilizing recent case law to support claims of implie…
What to monitor: Watch for further judicial interpretations regarding what constitutes 'reasonable' notice in diverse commercial sectors to refine licensing strategies and risk as…

news_items.title: Inside INTA London and the IP ‘carnival’
news_items.title_zh: INTA伦敦年会回顾：全球IP界的狂欢盛会
news_items.title_en: INTA London Annual Meeting: A Global IP Carnival Highlighting Trademark Trends
news_items.summary: Against a backdrop of rooftop receptions, hot-topic debates, hectic networking, and cross-city navigation, WIPR brings you highlights and key talking points from the world’s largest IP conference.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 世界知识产权评论（WIPR）回顾了在国际商标协会（INTA）于伦敦举办的年度会议。这是该会议时隔35年重返英国首都，吸引了来自180个国家的逾万名代表参加。尽管面临交通拥堵等挑战，与会者仍积极参与屋顶招待会及社交活动。文章强调了此次会议作为全球最大IP盛会的地位，突出了伦敦作为举办地的吸引力以及业界在商标领域的高热度讨论和网络交流机会。
news_items.ai_summary_en: World IP Review (WIPR) provides a comprehensive overview of the International Trademark Association (INTA) Annual Meeting held in London. This marked the first time the conference returned to the UK capital in over thirty-five years, drawing more than ten thousand delegates from one hundred and eighty countries. Despite logistical challenges such as transport disruptions, attendees actively engaged in rooftop receptions and extensive networking events. The article underscores the event's status as the premier global intellectual property gathering, emphasizing London’s appeal as a host city and highlighting the intense industry discussions and valuable networking opportunities within the trademark sector.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，英国后续是否会围绕这类规则动态给出配套安排或更明确口径。
news_items.ai_insight_en: Participation in premier international forums like INTA offers critical insights into evolving trademark policies across Europe and North America. Engaging with these platforms allows legal professionals and brand managers to anticipate regulatory shifts, thereby optimizing overseas portfolio strategies. Furthermore, cultivating robust international networks is essential for effectively managing cross-border infringement risks. These connections facilitate faster response mechanisms to enforcement issues, ultimately strengthening global compliance frameworks and enhancing brand influence in competitive international markets.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["INTA年会时隔35年重返伦敦，吸引180国逾万名代表参会", "会议涵盖商标热点辩论与高强度社交，展现行业活力", "尽管交通受阻，伦敦地标与社交活动仍极大提升了参会体验"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["INTA returned to London after thirty-five years, attracting over ten thousand representatives from one hundred and eighty nations.", "The conference featured heated debates on trademark hot topics and intensive social interactions, demonstrating strong industry vitality.", "Despite transport obstacles, London landmarks and vibrant social activities significantly enhanced the overall participant experience."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["利用顶级平台洞察欧美商标政策风向，优化海外布局", "拓展国际人脉以应对跨境侵权，提升品牌全球合规力"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Leverage top-tier platforms to gain early insights into European and US trademark policy trends for optimized overseas layouts.", "Practice note: Expand international professional networks to better address cross-border infringement challenges and enhance global compliance capabilities.", "What to monitor: Emerging regulatory discussions and enforcement priorities highlighted during major international IP conferences."]
core_points_en: INTA returned to London after thirty-five years, attracting over ten thousand representatives from one hundred and eighty nations.
The conference featured heated debates on trademark hot topics and intensive social interactions, demonstrating strong industry vitality.
Despite transport obstacles, London landmarks and vibrant social activities significantly enhanced the overall participant experience.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Leverage top-tier platforms to gain early insights into European and US trademark policy trends for optimized overseas layouts.
Practice note: Expand international professional networks to better address cross-border infringement challenges and enhance global compliance capabilities.
What to monitor: Emerging regulatory discussions and enforcement priorities highlighted during major international IP conferences.

news_items.title: HP prevails in Munich in toner cartridge dispute against Ninestar
news_items.title_zh: HP在慕尼黑墨盒专利纠纷中胜诉Ninestar
news_items.title_en: HP Secures Victory in Munich Toner Cartridge Patent Dispute Against Ninestar Group
news_items.summary: In a series of lawsuits concerning various patents, Hewlett Packard Development is taking action at Munich Regional Court against several European and Chinese companies of the Ninestar Group. They all sell printer cartridges on platforms such as Amazon. According to HP, these cartridges infringe HP’s patents. After the court imposed a sales ban on CaiLuo last […] The post HP prevails in Munich in toner cartridge dispute against Ninestar appeared first on JUVE Patent .
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 惠普开发公司在慕尼黑地区法院对纳思达集团多家欧中企业发起系列专利诉讼，指控其通过亚马逊等平台销售的打印机墨盒侵犯惠普专利。法院已对其中一家名为CaiLuo的公司实施销售禁令。此案反映了跨国企业在欧洲针对替代耗材市场的严格知识产权保护行动，凸显了当地司法机构在认定专利侵权及执行禁令方面的效率与力度。
news_items.ai_summary_en: Hewlett Packard Development has initiated a series of patent infringement lawsuits at the Munich Regional Court against multiple European and Chinese entities within the Ninestar Group. The litigation targets printer cartridges sold via online platforms such as Amazon, which HP alleges infringe its proprietary patents. In a significant development, the court has already imposed a sales ban on one of the defendants, CaiLuo. This case underscores the rigorous enforcement of intellectual property rights in Europe regarding the aftermarket consumables sector. It highlights the efficiency and decisive nature of local judicial authorities in determining patent infringement and executing preliminary injunctions, signaling a strict stance against unauthorized compatible products.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 当前更值得关注的是，德国后续是否会围绕这类执法动作给出配套安排或更明确口径。
news_items.ai_insight_en: This enforcement action illustrates the heightened risks for manufacturers and distributors of compatible consumables operating within the German market. Rights holders are increasingly leveraging the efficiency of German courts to secure rapid injunctive relief against alleged infringers. For industry participants, conducting thorough Freedom-to-Operate analyses prior to market entry is critical to mitigate exposure to such litigation. Furthermore, when facing infringement claims, relying solely on platform-level dispute mechanisms is insufficient; parties should actively engage in judicial proceedings and consider parallel invalidity actions to effectively defend their commercial interests.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["惠普在慕尼黑法院起诉纳思达集团侵犯多项专利", "涉案产品为通过亚马逊销售的打印机兼容墨盒", "法院已对部分被告实施临时销售禁令"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["HP filed multiple patent infringement suits in Munich against various Ninestar Group companies across Europe and China.", "The disputed products are compatible printer cartridges distributed through major e-commerce platforms like Amazon.", "The Munich Regional Court has issued temporary sales injunctions against specific defendants, including CaiLuo."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["出口企业需强化德国市场专利FTO尽职调查", "应对策略应结合无效宣告与积极应诉机制"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Manufacturers of compatible consumables must prioritize robust FTO due diligence before entering the German market to avoid costly injunctions.", "Practice note: Defense strategies should integrate active litigation participation with parallel invalidity proceedings rather than depending exclusively on e-commerce platform ap…", "What to monitor: Ongoing developments in this multi-party litigation and subsequent rulings by the Munich Regional Court regarding patent validity and infringement scope."]
core_points_en: HP filed multiple patent infringement suits in Munich against various Ninestar Group companies across Europe and China.
The disputed products are compatible printer cartridges distributed through major e-commerce platforms like Amazon.
The Munich Regional Court has issued temporary sales injunctions against specific defendants, including CaiLuo.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Manufacturers of compatible consumables must prioritize robust FTO due diligence before entering the German market to avoid costly injunctions.
Practice note: Defense strategies should integrate active litigation participation with parallel invalidity proceedings rather than depending exclusively on e-commerce platform ap…
What to monitor: Ongoing developments in this multi-party litigation and subsequent rulings by the Munich Regional Court regarding patent validity and infringement scope.

news_items.title: LD Helsinki, April 29, 2026, Decision On Infringement And Validity, UPC_CFI_214/2023, UPC_CFI_403/2025
news_items.title_zh: UPC赫尔辛基分院：专利侵权与有效性裁决及等同原则适用
news_items.title_en: UPC Helsinki Adopts Hague Equivalence Approach and Limits Revocation Review Scope
news_items.summary: The Helsinki Local Division of the Unified Patent Court examined infringement proceedings involving EP 3 295 663, with AIM Sport Development AG asserting patent rights against multiple TGI Sport entities across European jurisdictions. The case included a counterclaim for revocation, requiring the court to analyze validity under Articles 54, 56, and 123(2) EPC alongside infringement claims under Article 69 EPC.
news_items.ai_summary_zh: 统一专利法院（UPC）赫尔辛基地方分院就EP 3 295 663号专利作出侵权与有效性判决。在缺乏上诉法院先例时，法院采纳海牙分院的等同侵权判定路径，强调等同标准需累积满足，任一不成立即无侵权。针对撤销反诉，法院指出若当事人未充分论证“新增事项”，仅需回应已 substantiated 的部分。该案明确了UPC内部司法一致性的重要性及举证责任边界。
news_items.ai_summary_en: The Unified Patent Court’s Helsinki Local Division issued a decision on infringement and validity concerning EP 3 295 663. In proceedings brought by AIM Sport Development AG against multiple TGI Sport entities, the court addressed key procedural and substantive issues. Absent Court of Appeal precedent on equivalence, the Division adopted the approach established by The Hague Local Division to ensure legal certainty. The judgment clarified that equivalence criteria are cumulative; failure to meet any single criterion precludes a finding of infringement. Regarding the counterclaim for revocation, the court ruled it would only address allegations supported by substantiated arguments. Since the defendants provided minimal reasoning for their added matter claim, the court’s analysis was correspondingly limited, highlighting strict boundaries for party submissions.
news_items.ai_insight_zh: 中国企业在应对UPC诉讼时，需密切关注各地方分院对等同原则的司法倾向，尤其是海牙、赫尔辛基等关键分院。在提出无效抗辩时，必须提供详尽的技术比对与法律论证，避免仅凭形式主张导致被驳回。建议建立跨法域的专利风险预警机制，统一应诉策略以应对UPC日益增强的判例约束力。
news_items.ai_insight_en: This decision underscores the importance of judicial consistency among UPC Local Divisions, particularly regarding the doctrine of equivalents. Practitioners should note that while divisions may look to peers like The Hague for guidance, the cumulative nature of equivalence tests offers potential defense avenues. Furthermore, the ruling on Article 123(2) EPC emphasizes that formalistic invalidity claims lacking detailed technical or legal substantiation will receive limited judicial attention. Parties must ensure robust, evidence-based arguments in counterclaims to secure comprehensive court consideration, avoiding reliance on bare assertions that may be dismissed without full analysis.
news_items.ai_core_points_zh: ["无上诉先例时，地方法院参照海牙分院等同判定路径以保法律确定性", "等同侵权判定标准具有累积性，任一要件缺失即认定不构成等同侵权", "法院仅对当事人提供实质论证的撤销理由进行回应，简化审理焦点"]
news_items.ai_core_points_en: ["Local divisions may adopt peer division approaches, such as The Hague’s, to ensure legal certainty when no Court of Appeal precedent exists.", "Equivalence infringement criteria are cumulative; if any single criterion is unmet, the court need not assess remaining factors.", "Courts respond to revocation allegations only to the extent parties present substantiated arguments, limiting judicial review scope."]
news_items.ai_insight_points_zh: ["关注UPC地方分院等同原则司法倾向，统一跨法域应诉策略", "无效抗辩需提供实质技术论证，避免形式主张被法院忽略"]
news_items.ai_insight_points_en: ["Client relevance: Understand how Local Divisions harmonize equivalence assessments to predict litigation outcomes across jurisdictions.", "Practice note: Ensure invalidity counterclaims include detailed technical comparisons and legal arguments to avoid summary dismissal.", "What to monitor: Track evolving case law from key divisions like The Hague and Helsinki regarding cumulative equivalence criteria."]
core_points_en: Local divisions may adopt peer division approaches, such as The Hague’s, to ensure legal certainty when no Court of Appeal precedent exists.
Equivalence infringement criteria are cumulative; if any single criterion is unmet, the court need not assess remaining factors.
Courts respond to revocation allegations only to the extent parties present substantiated arguments, limiting judicial review scope.
insight_points_en: Client relevance: Understand how Local Divisions harmonize equivalence assessments to predict litigation outcomes across jurisdictions.
Practice note: Ensure invalidity counterclaims include detailed technical comparisons and legal arguments to avoid summary dismissal.
What to monitor: Track evolving case law from key divisions like The Hague and Helsinki regarding cumulative equivalence criteria.
